# PhantomOS v2 — agent context

Generated by `make docs-llm`. Single-file digest of curated docs for LLM agents.
Source of truth lives in the linked files; regenerate this file after doc changes.

5. docs/PLAN-phase-1-upgrades.md

---

## === README.md ===

# PhantomOS v2

PhantomOS is a Wails-based desktop app for managing AI coding sessions across multiple providers (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) with a config-driven provider architecture.

## Quick start

```bash
make frontend-install
make dev
```

See `Makefile` for all targets (`build`, `release-dmg`, `mcp-build`, etc.).

## Repository layout

- `cmd/` — Go entrypoints (Wails app, standalone MCP binary).
- `internal/` — Go backend: providers, app bindings, db (sqlc), git, terminal, tui.
- `frontend/` — SolidJS + Vanilla Extract + Kobalte UI.
- `docs/` — design docs, plans, research notes.
- `configs/` — provider config TOMLs.

## For agents

If you are an LLM/coding agent loading this repo, start with **`llms-full.txt`** at the repo root. It is a single-file digest of the curated docs (README, AI engine, config-driven architecture, home redesign plan, phase 1 upgrades plan) and is sized to fit in a single context window.

Regenerate it after doc changes:

```bash
make docs-llm
```

The file is committed to the repo so it can be fetched directly via raw URL.

## Authoritative docs

- `docs/ai-engine.md` — PhantomOS AI engine (graph, blast radius, orchestrator).
- `docs/DESIGN-config-driven-architecture.md` — provider abstraction.
- `docs/PLAN-home-redesign.md` — home screen redesign plan.
- `docs/PLAN-phase-1-upgrades.md` — current phase 1 PR plan.

## === docs/ai-engine.md ===

<!-- Author: Subash karki -->

# AI Engine

## 1. Overview

The PhantomOS AI engine watches the on-disk artifacts that AI coding CLIs (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex) write to `~/.claude/` and `~/.codex/`, normalizes them through a single `Provider` interface, and pushes typed events to the Solid.js frontend over Wails. It does not call the Anthropic API or Codex API directly for session data — every read is a filesystem operation against the CLI's own storage. The chat feature in `internal/chat/` does spawn the local `claude` CLI for streamed completions, but that is a separate subsystem from the session/streaming pipeline.

## 2. Provider system

The provider package decomposes an AI tool integration into six small interfaces (`internal/provider/provider.go:141-194`) — `ProviderIdentity`, `SessionDiscoverer`, `ConversationParser`, `CostCalculator`, `CommandRunner`, `PathResolver` — composed into `Provider` (`internal/provider/provider.go:204-215`). All shared shapes (`RawSession`, `Message`, `TokenUsage`, `HealthStatus`) live in the same file.

### 3-tier config loading

`Registry.LoadAll` (`internal/provider/registry.go:238-263`) loads configs in priority order:

1. Embedded YAML compiled into the binary via `internal/provider/embed.go` and `LoadFromEmbed` (`registry.go:89-127`). Errors here are fatal.
2. User overrides from `~/.phantom-os/providers/` via `LoadOverrides` (`registry.go:271-330`). Each override is deep-merged onto its matching builtin (`MergeConfigs`); parse or validation errors are logged and skipped.
3. Custom providers from `~/.phantom-os/providers/custom/` via `LoadFromDir` (`registry.go:51-86`). Errors are logged and skipped.

Both user directories are pre-created at boot by `EnsureConfigDir` (`internal/provider/init.go:19-32`).

### Registry, factories, instantiation

`Registry` (`internal/provider/registry.go:32-46`) holds three maps: `providers`, `configs`, `factories`. Go adapters self-register via `RegisterAdapterFactory(goPackage, fn)` (`registry.go:223-230`); `main.go:100-105` does this for `claude` and `codex`. After `LoadAll`, `InstantiateAll` (`registry.go:367-377`) walks every loaded config, looks up its `adapter.go_package`, and replaces the generic `ConfigProvider` with the adapter-built `Provider`. Configs without a registered factory keep the YAML-only `ConfigProvider`.

### Active provider selection

`main.go:115-126` picks the active provider with a hardcoded preference: `claude` if registered, otherwise the first entry from `Registry.Enabled()` (which filters to `Enabled() && IsInstalled()` in `registry.go:156-167`). If nothing is available the process exits.

## 3. Adapters

### Claude (`internal/provider/claude/claude.go`)

`ClaudeProvider` embeds `*provider.ConfigProvider` (`claude.go:27-34`) and overrides only what config cannot express:

- `FindConversationFile` (`claude.go:49-98`) implements Claude's encoded-path convention: `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-with-/-replaced-by->/<sessionID>.jsonl`. With an empty `cwd` it falls back to `filepath.WalkDir` across the conversations root.
- `ParseConversation` reads JSONL with nested content blocks (text, `tool_use`, `thinking`).

Everything else (paths, sessions glob, cost rules, command templates) is driven by `internal/provider/configs/claude.yaml`.

### Codex (`internal/provider/codex/codex.go`)

`CodexProvider` overrides four methods (`codex.go:1-12`):

- `DiscoverSessions` (`codex.go:53-65`) uses a dual strategy — query `~/.codex/state_5.sqlite` first via `discoverFromSQLite`, then fall back to the JSONL index (`discoverFromJSONLIndex`, `codex.go:155+`) if SQLite fails.
- `FindConversationFile` walks Codex's date-nested layout (`YYYY/MM/DD/`).
- `ParseConversation` decodes the OpenAI event schema (`session_meta`, `event_msg`, `response_item`, `turn_context`).
- `ParseUsage` maps OpenAI token fields (`prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`, `total_tokens`) onto `TokenUsage`.

The boundary is consistent: anything that can be expressed as paths, globs, regex, or pricing tiers stays in YAML; anything that requires reading a foreign data structure (Claude's nested blocks, Codex's SQLite, Codex's event schema) lives in the adapter.

## 4. Session lifecycle

The collector registry (`internal/collector/registry.go:14-25`) manages five collectors that all implement `Collector` (`internal/collector/collector.go:10-14`). `main.go:146-180` wires them up; `Registry.StartAll` (`registry.go:35-88`) launches each in a goroutine with a 5-second startup timeout.

| Collector | File | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `SessionWatcher` | `session_watcher.go:39-72` | fsnotify-watches `prov.SessionsDir()`, debounces writes 200ms, upserts sessions, runs a 5s stale detector and 10s context bridge |
| `JSONLScanner` | `jsonl_scanner.go:39-58` | Initial full scan of conversation JSONL, plus 60s periodic rescan for sessions still missing tokens (`rescanMaxAttempts = 20`, `jsonl_scanner.go:35`) and a 10s active-context poller |
| `ActivityPoller` | `activity_poller.go:61-70` | fsnotify on per-project directories; emits `EventActivity` batches as new lines append to JSONL |
| `TaskWatcher` | `task_watcher.go:51-66` | Watches `prov.TasksDir()` recursively (`addSubdirectories`, `task_watcher.go:130`), backfills session names |
| `TodoWatcher` | `todo_watcher.go:45-60` | Watches `prov.TodosDir()`, derives `sessionID` from filename (`extractSessionID`, `todo_watcher.go:132`) |
| `SessionEnricher` | `session_enricher.go:32-40` | Not in the registry — runs `StartPeriodicEnrichment` directly (`main.go:162`); also called inline by `SessionWatcher.handleRemove` (`session_watcher.go:447`) |

### Linker — terminal binding

`linker.Linker` (`internal/linker/linker.go:24-38`) couples terminal panes to AI sessions. `LinkSessionToUnlinkedTerminals` (`linker.go:105-168`) is invoked from `SessionWatcher` after a new active session is upserted (`session_watcher.go:415-419`). It filters by CWD overlap (`CWDsMatch`), then disambiguates with `IsDescendant` PID ancestry when multiple terminal candidates match (`linker.go:129-144`). The reverse direction — terminal-first — is `LinkTerminalToActiveSession` (`linker.go:43-101`), which falls back to most-recent on PID failure. `UnlinkSession` (`linker.go:203-227`) is called from `SessionWatcher.handleRemove` (`session_watcher.go:441`).

The collector→linker dependency is one-way; `SessionWatcher` references the linker through a local `terminalLinker` interface (`session_watcher.go:27-30`) to avoid an import cycle.

### Auto-tail flow

`main.go:191-213` builds the cross-cutting glue between `SessionWatcher` and `stream.Service`:

```
sessionWatcher.SetOnActive(fn)
  → activeProv.FindConversationFile(sessionID, "")
  → streamSvc.StartTailing(ctx, sessionID, jsonlPath)
```

`SetOnActive` (`session_watcher.go:78-80`) is fired from `processSessionFile` (`session_watcher.go:389-391`) whenever a session is upserted with `status == "active"`. The `tailedSessions` map in `main.go:191-200` deduplicates so a single session is tailed exactly once across watcher restarts.

## 5. Streaming

`stream.Service` (`internal/stream/service.go:18-32`) wraps a `Store` (DB writer) and a `sync.Map` of active tailers keyed by `sessionID`. Two operations matter:

- `ParseSession` (`service.go:36-56`) — one-shot: `Scanner.ScanAll` reads the whole JSONL, batched into `Store.SaveBatch`, then a single `stream:batch` event.
- `StartTailing` (`service.go:61-103`) — spins up a producer goroutine running `Scanner.Tail` (`internal/stream/scanner.go:84-156`) and a consumer goroutine. The producer uses fsnotify on the parent directory (more reliable cross-platform than per-file, `scanner.go:102-108`) with debounce + 500ms ticker fallback (`scanner.go:110-155`); if fsnotify is unavailable it falls back to `tailPoll` (`scanner.go:97-99`, `scanner.go:176`). The consumer persists each event with `Store.SaveEvent`, optionally invokes the registered `EventHook`, then emits `stream:event`. `StartTailing` is idempotent: a `LoadOrStore` guard at `service.go:62-65` makes a second call for the same session a no-op.

### Event model

`stream.Event` (`internal/stream/event.go:18-54`) is a flat struct covering all event kinds via `EventType` (`event.go:6-16`): `thinking`, `tool_use`, `tool_result`, `assistant`, `user`, `error`, `system`. Token fields, model, file path, diff content, and `SeqNum` are inline. `Timeline` and `TimelinePoint` (`event.go:57-72`) provide the condensed view.

The parser (`internal/stream/parser.go`) supports both single-event (`ParseLine`, `parser.go:49`) and multi-event (`ParseLineMulti`, `parser.go:217`) extraction — needed because a single Claude assistant JSONL line can contain multiple content blocks (text + tool_use + thinking).

## 6. Chat service

`chat.Service` (`internal/chat/service.go:31-45`) is independent from the session/stream pipeline. It manages persistent conversations + messages in SQLite and streams replies by spawning the local `claude` CLI — not by hitting the Anthropic Messages API directly.

`SendMessage` (`service.go:189-237`) executes:

1. `exec.LookPath("claude")` (`service.go:195`); if missing, emit `chat:stream` error with install hint.
2. Persist user message (`SaveMessage`).
3. Build prompt from history (`buildPrompt`, `service.go:246`).
4. `streamFromClaude` (`service.go:282-369`) runs `claude -p <prompt> --output-format stream-json --verbose --model <m> --no-session-persistence --dangerously-skip-permissions --setting-sources ""` with `cmd.Dir` set to a fresh `os.MkdirTemp("", "phantom-chat-*")` — explicitly preventing CLAUDE.md / `.claude/` discovery from leaking workspace context (`service.go:297-304`).
5. Parse NDJSON from stdout, dispatch through `handleStreamEvent` (`service.go:381+`), accumulate text into `fullContent`.
6. Persist assistant message and emit `chat:stream` `done`.

Stream events (`internal/chat/types.go`): `delta`, `done`, `error`, `thinking`, `tool_use`.

## 7. Frontend integration

| Binding file (`frontend/src/core/bindings/`) | Wails methods exposed |
|---|---|
| `sessions.ts` | `GetSessions`, `GetActiveSessions`, `GetSession`, `GetSessionTasks`, `GetActivityLog`, `ParseSessionHistory`, `PauseSession`, `ResumeSession`, `KillSession` |
| `providers.ts` | `GetProviders`, `GetProviderDetail`, `SetProviderEnabled`, `SetActiveProvider`, `TestProvider`, `AutoDetectProviders`, `AddCustomProvider`, `RemoveCustomProvider`, `ResetProviderOverride`, `GetActiveProvider` |
| `chat.ts` | `GetConversations`, `CreateConversation`, `DeleteConversation`, `SendChatMessage`, `GetChatHistory` |
| `journal.ts` | session-derived daily aggregates produced by `SessionEnricher` |

All bindings call through `(window as any).go.app.App` — Wails' generated bridge.

### Wails events emitted by the AI engine

Constants in `internal/app/events.go` and `internal/collector/events.go`:

| Event | Source | Payload |
|---|---|---|
| `session:new` | `session_watcher.go:380` | `{sessionId, cwd, kind, status}` |
| `session:update` | `session_watcher.go:358,717` | `{sessionId, status, ...}` |
| `session:end` | `session_watcher.go:456` | `{sessionId, reason}` |
| `session:stale` | `session_watcher.go:544` | `{sessionId, ...}` |
| `session:context` | `session_watcher.go:678`, `jsonl_scanner.go:607` | session context-window stats |
| `activity` | `activity_poller.go:274` | `[]activityEvent` |
| `jsonl:rescan` | `jsonl_scanner.go:434` | `{sessionId, ...}` |
| `jsonl:scan-complete` | declared `events.go:15`, `app/events.go:26` | scan summary |
| `task:new` | `task_watcher.go:247`, `todo_watcher.go:203` | `{sessionId, taskId, ...}` |
| `task:update` | `task_watcher.go:271`, `todo_watcher.go:223,262` | `{sessionId, taskId, ...}` |
| `terminal:linked` | `linker.go:95,162` | `{paneId, sessionId}` |
| `terminal:unlinked` | `linker.go:193,221` | `{paneId, sessionId}` |
| `stream:batch` | `stream/service.go:50` | `{session_id, count}` |
| `stream:event` | `stream/service.go:89` | `Event` |
| `chat:stream` | `chat/service.go:197,220,234,396+` | `StreamEvent` |
| `journal:eod-trigger` | `session_watcher.go:738` | nil |

`app.EmitEvent` (`internal/app/events.go:54-56`) is a one-line wrapper around `wailsRuntime.EventsEmit`. Every collector receives `func(string, interface{})` closures from `main.go` so they never import Wails directly.

The Solid.js consumers live under `frontend/src/core/` (signals + types) and `frontend/src/features/` — out of scope for this document.

## 8. Data flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant ClaudeCLI as Claude CLI
    participant FS as ~/.claude/sessions + ~/.claude/projects
    participant SW as SessionWatcher
    participant LK as Linker
    participant SS as stream.Service
    participant Wails as Wails runtime
    participant FE as Frontend (Solid)

    User->>ClaudeCLI: claude
    ClaudeCLI->>FS: write sessions/SID.json
    FS-->>SW: fsnotify Create/Write
    SW->>SW: debounce 200ms, upsert DB
    SW->>Wails: emit session:new
    Wails->>FE: session:new
    SW->>LK: LinkSessionToUnlinkedTerminals(SID, cwd, pid)
    LK->>Wails: emit terminal:linked
    SW->>SS: onActive(SID) -> FindConversationFile -> StartTailing
    ClaudeCLI->>FS: append to projects/.../SID.jsonl
    FS-->>SS: fsnotify on parent dir
    SS->>SS: Scanner.Tail -> parse line
    SS->>Wails: emit stream:event
    Wails->>FE: stream:event
    User->>ClaudeCLI: exit
    ClaudeCLI->>FS: remove sessions/SID.json
    FS-->>SW: fsnotify Remove
    SW->>LK: UnlinkSession(SID)
    SW->>Wails: emit session:end
```

## 9. File map

| Concern | Path | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Provider interface | `internal/provider/provider.go` | Six focused interfaces composed into `Provider`; normalized data types |
| Config-driven default | `internal/provider/config_provider.go` | Generic `ConfigProvider` that satisfies `Provider` from YAML alone |
| Registry + 3-tier load | `internal/provider/registry.go` | `LoadFromEmbed`, `LoadOverrides`, `LoadFromDir`, `InstantiateAll`, factories |
| Embedded configs | `internal/provider/embed.go`, `internal/provider/configs/*.yaml` | `claude.yaml`, `codex.yaml`, `gemini.yaml` |
| Boot config dirs | `internal/provider/init.go` | `EnsureConfigDir` |
| Claude adapter | `internal/provider/claude/claude.go` | Encoded-path JSONL discovery, nested-block parser |
| Codex adapter | `internal/provider/codex/codex.go` | SQLite + JSONL-index discovery, OpenAI event parser |
| Wiring | `main.go` | Adapter factories, `LoadAll`/`InstantiateAll`, active selection, collector wiring, auto-tail glue |
| Collector interface | `internal/collector/collector.go` | `Name()/Start()/Stop()` |
| Collector registry | `internal/collector/registry.go` | Lifecycle manager with 5s startup timeout, reverse-order shutdown |
| Session lifecycle | `internal/collector/session_watcher.go` | fsnotify on sessions dir, stale detection, journal hooks |
| Conversation enrichment | `internal/collector/jsonl_scanner.go` | Token/cost computation, periodic rescan, active context polling |
| Activity events | `internal/collector/activity_poller.go` | Per-project JSONL deltas |
| Tasks/todos | `internal/collector/task_watcher.go`, `internal/collector/todo_watcher.go` | Watch `tasks/` and `todos/` dirs |
| Session post-processing | `internal/collector/session_enricher.go` | Summary, files-touched, git stats, daily aggregates |
| Backfill | `internal/collector/backfill.go` | One-shot `BackfillJournal` for `--backfill-journal` mode (`main.go:38-44`) |
| Collector events | `internal/collector/events.go` | Event-name constants |
| App-level events | `internal/app/events.go` | Wails event constants + `EmitEvent` wrapper |
| Terminal binding | `internal/linker/linker.go` | CWD overlap + PID ancestry, link/unlink |
| Linker helpers | `internal/linker/cwd.go`, `internal/linker/pid.go` | `CWDsMatch`, `IsDescendant` |
| Stream coordination | `internal/stream/service.go` | `ParseSession`, `StartTailing`, `StopTailing`, `EventHook` |
| JSONL scanning | `internal/stream/scanner.go` | `ScanAll`, `ScanFrom`, `Tail` (fsnotify + poll fallback) |
| JSONL parsing | `internal/stream/parser.go` | `ParseLine`, `ParseLineMulti` |
| Stream events | `internal/stream/event.go` | `Event`, `EventType`, `Timeline` |
| Persistence | `internal/stream/store.go` | `SaveEvent`, `SaveBatch`, `GetEvents`, `GetTimeline` |
| Chat (CLI-spawn) | `internal/chat/service.go`, `internal/chat/types.go` | Conversations + `claude` CLI streaming |
| Frontend bindings | `frontend/src/core/bindings/sessions.ts`, `providers.ts`, `chat.ts`, `journal.ts` | Typed Wails RPC wrappers |

## 10. Gotchas

- **Hardcoded active provider preference.** `main.go:115-118` always picks Claude when registered, regardless of any "preferred provider" config. Selection only flips to Codex (or anything else) when Claude is absent.
- **Backfill is a separate process mode.** `--backfill-journal` (`main.go:38-44`, `main.go:70-77`) reuses the DB and journal but `os.Exit(0)`s before Wails starts. The `journalSvc` and DB are still constructed before this branch — order matters.
- **`SessionEnricher` is not in the registry.** It's started directly by `go enricher.StartPeriodicEnrichment(...)` (`main.go:162`) and also called inline from `SessionWatcher.handleRemove` (`session_watcher.go:447`). It does not implement `Collector`, so it has no `Stop()` and is not gracefully shut down by `Registry.StopAll`.
- **`Registry.StartAll` reports startup before `Start()` actually returns.** `collector/registry.go:53-65` signals success as soon as the goroutine begins executing, then calls `Start(ctx)` which is blocking. A collector that fails inside `Start` after the signal is logged but does not abort startup.
- **Override files for unknown providers are silently skipped.** `LoadOverrides` (`registry.go:305-309`) only merges onto an existing builtin. Dropping a YAML for a brand-new provider into `~/.phantom-os/providers/` (instead of `providers/custom/`) does nothing but emit a warning.
- **`Adapter.go_package` is the only registry key.** A YAML pointing at a `go_package` no factory has registered for silently falls back to `ConfigProvider` (`registry.go:343-345`). There is no error or warning, so adapter mismatches surface only as missing behavior at runtime.
- **`StartTailing` deduplicates by storing `nil` first.** `service.go:62-65` uses `LoadOrStore(sessionID, nil)`; the real `cancel` is stored a line later. A racing `StopTailing` between those two lines would no-op silently because the stored value is still `nil`.
- **Auto-tail dedup map (`tailedSessions`, `main.go:191`) is per-process.** A session that ends and immediately restarts with the same ID inside one run will not be re-tailed. App restart resets the map, so this only matters for long-lived sessions that recycle their ID.
- **Chat service runs the `claude` CLI in a fresh tempdir.** `service.go:299-304` deliberately points `cmd.Dir` at `os.MkdirTemp` so user CLAUDE.md / `.claude/` files do not leak. If `MkdirTemp` fails, `cmd.Dir` is left unset and the CLI inherits the PhantomOS process CWD instead — potentially loading PhantomOS's own project context.
- **fsnotify is best-effort.** `stream/scanner.go:97-99` and the activity poller silently fall back to polling when fsnotify is unavailable. Behavior is correct, but the latency floor jumps from milliseconds to `tailFallbackInterval` (500ms in scanner, 30s in `jsonl_scanner.activeContextFallbackInterval`).

## === docs/DESIGN-config-driven-architecture.md ===

# Design: Config-Driven AI-Agnostic Architecture

**Author:** Subash Karki  
**Date:** 2026-04-27  
**Status:** Draft — Extends RESEARCH-ai-agnostic-architecture.md  
**Context:** Incorporates patterns from [Superset](https://github.com/superset-sh/superset) while going deeper on data integration.

---

## 1. Design Philosophy

Superset solved AI-agnostic orchestration at the **terminal layer** — agents are black-box CLI processes. This is elegant but shallow: it can launch any agent, but can't parse sessions, track tokens, calculate costs, or monitor activity.

Phantom OS needs to go deeper — parsing artifacts, normalizing data, tracking costs — while keeping the same config-driven simplicity. The goal:

> **Any new AI tool should be supportable by writing a YAML file. Complex tools get a Go adapter. Zero recompilation for config-only changes.**

### What We Take From Superset

| Pattern | Superset | Phantom OS Adaptation |
|---------|----------|----------------------|
| Agent definition registry | `BUILTIN_TERMINAL_AGENTS` array | `providers/` directory with YAML + Go combo |
| 3-layer resolution | builtin → override → custom | builtin YAML → user YAML → Go adapter |
| Prompt transport abstraction | `argv`, `stdin`, `file` | Same, plus `socket` for MCP-native tools |
| Override-only persistence | Only store diffs from defaults | Same — user YAML only contains overrides |
| Runtime env resolution | `ModelProviderRuntimeResolver` | Expanded: env vars + keychain + OAuth |
| Custom agent definitions | `AgentCustomDefinition` schema | `~/.phantom-os/providers/custom/*.yaml` |

### Where We Go Further

| Capability | Superset | Phantom OS |
|------------|----------|------------|
| Session parsing | None (terminal-only) | Config-driven field mapping + Go parsers |
| Token tracking | None | Declarative `usage_fields` mapping in YAML |
| Cost calculation | None | Pricing tiers in YAML, hot-reloadable |
| Activity monitoring | None | Configurable artifact dirs + fsnotify patterns |
| Provider auto-discovery | None | Scan known paths, detect installed tools |
| Provider health | None | Liveness probes (process check, path exists, CLI responds) |
| Multi-provider merging | None (one agent per workspace) | Unified session timeline across providers |
| Community marketplace | None | Hosted YAML registry for community providers |

---

## 2. Architecture Overview

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Phantom OS v2                       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                       │
│  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐ │
│  │ Provider     │  │ Provider     │  │ Provider     │ │
│  │ Registry     │  │ Auto-Detect  │  │ Health Mon   │ │
│  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬───────┘ │
│         │                │                 │          │
│  ┌──────▼─────────────────▼─────────────────▼──────┐ │
│  │              Provider Interface                  │ │
│  │  (Go interface + YAML config = full provider)    │ │
│  └──────┬───────────┬──────────────┬───────────────┘ │
│         │           │              │                  │
│  ┌──────▼──┐ ┌──────▼──┐  ┌───────▼──────┐          │
│  │ Claude  │ │ Codex   │  │ Config-Only  │          │
│  │ Adapter │ │ Adapter │  │ (YAML-only)  │          │
│  │ (Go+YAML│ │ (Go+YAML│  │ (Aider,etc)  │          │
│  └─────────┘ └─────────┘  └──────────────┘          │
│                                                       │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │              Normalized Data Layer                │ │
│  │  RawSession | TokenUsage | Message | Activity    │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                       │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │         SQLite → Frontend (SolidJS)              │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 3. Config Schema (Full Specification)

### 3.1 Provider Config — `~/.phantom-os/providers/{name}.yaml`

```yaml
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Provider Identity
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
provider: claude                     # unique ID, used as registry key
display_name: "Claude Code"
icon: "anthropic"                    # icon key for frontend
version: "1.0.0"                     # config schema version
enabled: true

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Auto-Detection — how Phantom discovers this tool
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
detection:
  # Check if the CLI binary exists
  binary: "claude"
  # Check if artifact directories exist
  paths:
    - "~/.claude/sessions/"
    - "~/.claude/projects/"
  # Minimum version (optional)
  version_command: ["claude", "--version"]
  version_pattern: "claude-code/(\d+\.\d+\.\d+)"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Artifact Paths — where this tool stores data
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
paths:
  sessions: "~/.claude/sessions/"
  conversations: "~/.claude/projects/"
  todos: "~/.claude/todos/"
  tasks: "~/.claude/tasks/"
  settings: "~/.claude/settings.json"
  context: "~/.phantom-os/context/claude/"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Session Discovery — how to find and parse session files
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
sessions:
  # File discovery
  glob: "**/*.json"
  exclude: ["*.lock", "*.tmp"]

  # JSON field mapping → RawSession
  fields:
    id: "sessionId"                  # JSONPath to session ID
    id_fallback: "id"               # fallback field name
    pid: "pid"
    cwd: "cwd"
    model: "model"
    started_at: "startedAt"
    name: "name"                     # optional display name
    kind: "type"                     # optional session type

  # Liveness check
  alive_check: "pid"                 # "pid" | "socket" | "api" | "none"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Conversation Parsing — how to read conversation logs
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
conversations:
  # Format
  encoding: "jsonl"                  # "jsonl" | "json" | "markdown" | "sqlite"
  file_extension: ".jsonl"

  # How CWD maps to conversation file path
  # "encoded" = Claude's URL-encoded path convention
  # "flat" = session ID as filename
  # "hash" = SHA256 of CWD
  # "nested" = mirror directory structure
  path_convention: "encoded"

  # Message type detection — maps provider types → normalized types
  message_types:
    user:
      match:
        - { field: "type", value: "human" }
        - { field: "role", value: "user" }
    assistant:
      match:
        - { field: "type", value: "assistant" }
        - { field: "role", value: "assistant" }
    tool_use:
      match:
        - { field: "type", value: "tool_use" }
    tool_result:
      match:
        - { field: "type", value: "tool_result" }
    system:
      match:
        - { field: "type", value: "system" }
    thinking:
      match:
        - { field: "type", value: "thinking" }

  # Token usage field mapping
  usage:
    # Where to find the usage block in each JSON line
    locations:
      - "usage"
      - "message.usage"
      - "result.usage"
    # Field name mapping → TokenUsage
    fields:
      input: "input_tokens"
      output: "output_tokens"
      cache_read: "cache_read_input_tokens"
      cache_write: "cache_creation_input_tokens"

  # Content extraction
  content:
    # Where the text content lives in a message
    text_paths:
      - "content"                    # direct string
      - "content[*].text"            # array of content blocks
      - "message.content[*].text"
    # Tool name extraction
    tool_name_path: "content[*].name"
    tool_input_path: "content[*].input"

  # Tool catalog — known tool names for this provider
  known_tools:
    - "Read"
    - "Edit"
    - "Write"
    - "Bash"
    - "Grep"
    - "Glob"
    - "Agent"
    - "Skill"
    - "WebFetch"
    - "WebSearch"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Commands — CLI commands with variable interpolation
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
commands:
  resume: "claude --resume --session-id ${SESSION_ID}"
  new_session: "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
  ai_generate: "claude --print -p '${PROMPT}'"

  # Prompt transport (from Superset) — how to pass prompts to the CLI
  prompt_transport: "argv"           # "argv" | "stdin" | "file" | "socket"

  # For file transport — where to write the prompt file
  prompt_file_pattern: ".phantom-os/task-${TASK_ID}.md"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Pricing — token cost calculation
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
pricing:
  # Remote pricing URL (auto-updates)
  remote_url: null                   # "https://pricing.phantom-os.dev/claude.json"
  update_interval: "24h"

  default_tier: "sonnet"
  tiers:
    sonnet:
      match: "sonnet"                # substring match against model name
      input_per_m: 3.0               # USD per million tokens
      output_per_m: 15.0
      cache_read_per_m: 0.30
      cache_write_per_m: 3.75
    opus:
      match: "opus"
      input_per_m: 15.0
      output_per_m: 75.0
      cache_read_per_m: 1.50
      cache_write_per_m: 18.75
    haiku:
      match: "haiku"
      input_per_m: 0.80
      output_per_m: 4.0
      cache_read_per_m: 0.08
      cache_write_per_m: 1.0

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Auth — how to resolve API credentials
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
auth:
  type: "env"                        # "env" | "keychain" | "oauth" | "config_file"
  env_vars:
    - "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"
    - "ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN"
  # For config_file type
  config_path: null
  # For keychain type
  keychain_service: null

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Safety — provider-specific safety ward patterns
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
safety:
  # Patterns that trigger safety warnings in conversation content
  dangerous_patterns:
    - pattern: "rm -rf /"
      severity: "critical"
    - pattern: "sudo"
      severity: "warning"
    - pattern: "--no-verify"
      severity: "info"

  # Max cost per session before alerting (microdollars)
  cost_alert_threshold: 5000000      # $5.00

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Adapter — when YAML isn't enough
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
adapter:
  # Go adapter for complex parsing logic
  # null = pure config-driven (generic JSONL/JSON/Markdown parser)
  # "claude" = use internal/provider/claude/ Go code
  go_package: "claude"

  # Which methods the Go adapter overrides (rest use generic config-driven logic)
  overrides:
    - "ParseConversation"            # Claude's nested content blocks need Go code
    - "FindConversationFile"         # Claude's encoded-path convention needs Go code
```

### 3.2 User Override — `~/.phantom-os/overrides/{name}.yaml`

Like Superset's preset overrides — only stores diffs from the builtin config.

```yaml
# Override just what you need — everything else inherits from builtin
provider: claude

# Change the default permission mode
commands:
  new_session: "claude --permission-mode acceptEdits"

# Add a custom pricing tier
pricing:
  tiers:
    sonnet-fast:
      match: "sonnet"
      input_per_m: 3.0
      output_per_m: 15.0

# Disable safety for local dev
safety:
  cost_alert_threshold: null
```

### 3.3 Custom Provider — `~/.phantom-os/providers/custom/{name}.yaml`

Users can define entirely new providers with zero Go code for tools that use standard JSONL/JSON formats:

```yaml
provider: my-local-llm
display_name: "My Local LLM"
icon: "custom"
enabled: true

detection:
  binary: "my-llm"
  paths:
    - "~/.my-llm/sessions/"

paths:
  sessions: "~/.my-llm/sessions/"
  conversations: "~/.my-llm/conversations/"

sessions:
  glob: "*.json"
  fields:
    id: "session_id"
    cwd: "working_dir"
    model: "model_name"
    started_at: "created_at"
  alive_check: "pid"

conversations:
  encoding: "jsonl"
  file_extension: ".jsonl"
  path_convention: "flat"

  message_types:
    user:
      match:
        - { field: "role", value: "user" }
    assistant:
      match:
        - { field: "role", value: "assistant" }

  usage:
    locations:
      - "usage"
    fields:
      input: "prompt_tokens"
      output: "completion_tokens"

commands:
  new_session: "my-llm chat"
  prompt_transport: "stdin"

pricing:
  default_tier: "default"
  tiers:
    default:
      match: "*"
      input_per_m: 0.0
      output_per_m: 0.0

# No adapter — pure config-driven parsing
adapter:
  go_package: null
```

---

## 4. Go Architecture

### 4.1 Provider Interface (Updated from Research)

```go
package provider

import (
    "context"
    "io"
)

// Provider defines what every AI tool integration must satisfy.
// Methods marked "config-driven" have a generic implementation that reads
// from YAML config. Go adapters only override methods that need custom logic.
type Provider interface {
    // Identity (config-driven)
    Name() string
    DisplayName() string
    Icon() string
    Enabled() bool

    // Detection (config-driven)
    IsInstalled() bool
    DetectedVersion() string

    // Session Discovery (config-driven for standard JSON)
    SessionsDir() string
    DiscoverSessions(ctx context.Context) ([]RawSession, error)
    IsSessionAlive(session RawSession) bool

    // Conversation Parsing (adapter override for complex formats)
    ConversationDir() string
    FindConversationFile(sessionID, cwd string) string
    ParseConversation(r io.Reader) (*ConversationData, error)

    // Token & Cost (config-driven)
    ParseUsage(raw map[string]any) *TokenUsage
    CalculateCost(model string, usage TokenUsage) int64

    // Commands (config-driven)
    ResumeCommand(sessionID string) string
    NewSessionCommand(cwd string) string
    AIGenerateCommand(prompt string) string
    PromptTransport() PromptTransport

    // Extra artifact dirs (config-driven)
    ExtraDirs() map[string]string

    // Auth (config-driven)
    HasUsableCredentials() bool
    PrepareEnv() map[string]string

    // Health
    HealthCheck(ctx context.Context) HealthStatus
}

type PromptTransport string

const (
    PromptArgv   PromptTransport = "argv"
    PromptStdin  PromptTransport = "stdin"
    PromptFile   PromptTransport = "file"
    PromptSocket PromptTransport = "socket"
)

type HealthStatus struct {
    Installed   bool
    Reachable   bool   // CLI responds to --version
    HasAuth     bool   // credentials found
    Version     string
    Error       string
}
```

### 4.2 Config-Driven Base Provider

```go
package provider

// ConfigProvider implements Provider using only YAML config.
// Works for any tool with standard JSON sessions + JSONL conversations.
// Specific adapters (Claude, Codex) embed this and override methods.
type ConfigProvider struct {
    config *ProviderConfig  // parsed from YAML
}

// NewConfigProvider creates a pure config-driven provider.
func NewConfigProvider(config *ProviderConfig) *ConfigProvider {
    return &ConfigProvider{config: config}
}

// All config-driven methods delegate to config fields:
func (p *ConfigProvider) Name() string        { return p.config.Provider }
func (p *ConfigProvider) DisplayName() string { return p.config.DisplayName }
func (p *ConfigProvider) Icon() string        { return p.config.Icon }
func (p *ConfigProvider) Enabled() bool       { return p.config.Enabled }

func (p *ConfigProvider) SessionsDir() string {
    return expandPath(p.config.Paths.Sessions)
}

func (p *ConfigProvider) DiscoverSessions(ctx context.Context) ([]RawSession, error) {
    // Generic: glob for files, parse JSON using field mappings from config
    return discoverSessionsFromConfig(ctx, p.config)
}

func (p *ConfigProvider) ParseUsage(raw map[string]any) *TokenUsage {
    // Generic: read fields using config.Conversations.Usage.Fields mapping
    return parseUsageFromConfig(raw, p.config)
}

func (p *ConfigProvider) CalculateCost(model string, usage TokenUsage) int64 {
    // Generic: match model against pricing tiers, calculate microdollars
    return calculateCostFromConfig(model, usage, p.config)
}

// ... other config-driven methods
```

### 4.3 Claude Adapter (Overrides Complex Methods)

```go
package claude

import (
    "phantom-os/internal/provider"
)

// ClaudeProvider extends ConfigProvider with Claude-specific parsing.
type ClaudeProvider struct {
    *provider.ConfigProvider
}

func New(config *provider.ProviderConfig) *ClaudeProvider {
    return &ClaudeProvider{
        ConfigProvider: provider.NewConfigProvider(config),
    }
}

// Override: Claude uses encoded-path convention for conversation files
func (p *ClaudeProvider) FindConversationFile(sessionID, cwd string) string {
    // Claude encodes the CWD as a URL-safe directory name
    return findClaudeConversationFile(p.ConversationDir(), sessionID, cwd)
}

// Override: Claude's nested content blocks need custom parsing
func (p *ClaudeProvider) ParseConversation(r io.Reader) (*provider.ConversationData, error) {
    // Claude JSONL has nested content arrays, thinking blocks, etc.
    return parseClaudeConversation(r, p.Config())
}
```

### 4.4 Provider Registry

```go
package provider

import (
    "os"
    "path/filepath"
    "gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
)

type Registry struct {
    providers map[string]Provider
    configs   map[string]*ProviderConfig
    watcher   *fsnotify.Watcher    // hot-reload on config change
}

func NewRegistry() *Registry {
    r := &Registry{
        providers: make(map[string]Provider),
        configs:   make(map[string]*ProviderConfig),
    }
    return r
}

// LoadAll reads builtin + user + custom provider configs
func (r *Registry) LoadAll() error {
    // 1. Load builtin configs (embedded in binary)
    for _, cfg := range embeddedConfigs {
        r.configs[cfg.Provider] = cfg
    }

    // 2. Load user overrides (deep merge onto builtins)
    overrideDir := expandPath("~/.phantom-os/overrides/")
    r.loadOverrides(overrideDir)

    // 3. Load custom providers
    customDir := expandPath("~/.phantom-os/providers/custom/")
    r.loadCustomProviders(customDir)

    // 4. Instantiate providers
    for name, cfg := range r.configs {
        r.providers[name] = r.instantiate(cfg)
    }

    // 5. Start hot-reload watcher
    r.startWatcher()

    return nil
}

// instantiate creates the right provider based on adapter config
func (r *Registry) instantiate(cfg *ProviderConfig) Provider {
    if cfg.Adapter.GoPackage == "" {
        // Pure config-driven — no Go adapter needed
        return NewConfigProvider(cfg)
    }

    // Dispatch to Go adapter
    switch cfg.Adapter.GoPackage {
    case "claude":
        return claude.New(cfg)
    case "codex":
        return codex.New(cfg)
    default:
        return NewConfigProvider(cfg)
    }
}

// Enabled returns all enabled providers
func (r *Registry) Enabled() []Provider {
    var result []Provider
    for _, p := range r.providers {
        if p.Enabled() && p.IsInstalled() {
            result = append(result, p)
        }
    }
    return result
}

// Get returns a specific provider by name
func (r *Registry) Get(name string) (Provider, bool) {
    p, ok := r.providers[name]
    return p, ok
}
```

---

## 5. Auto-Discovery System

Something Superset doesn't do at all — Phantom detects which tools are installed and auto-enables them.

```go
// AutoDetect scans the system for known AI tools and returns detected providers
func (r *Registry) AutoDetect() []DetectionResult {
    var results []DetectionResult

    for name, cfg := range r.configs {
        result := DetectionResult{
            Provider: name,
            Display:  cfg.DisplayName,
        }

        // Check binary
        if cfg.Detection.Binary != "" {
            path, err := exec.LookPath(cfg.Detection.Binary)
            result.BinaryFound = err == nil
            result.BinaryPath = path
        }

        // Check artifact paths
        for _, p := range cfg.Detection.Paths {
            expanded := expandPath(p)
            if _, err := os.Stat(expanded); err == nil {
                result.PathsFound = append(result.PathsFound, expanded)
            }
        }

        // Check version
        if result.BinaryFound && cfg.Detection.VersionCommand != nil {
            out, err := exec.Command(cfg.Detection.VersionCommand[0],
                cfg.Detection.VersionCommand[1:]...).Output()
            if err == nil {
                result.Version = extractVersion(string(out), cfg.Detection.VersionPattern)
            }
        }

        result.Installed = result.BinaryFound && len(result.PathsFound) > 0
        results = append(results, result)
    }

    return results
}
```

### First-Run Experience

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         Phantom OS — First Run Setup         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                              │
│  Detected AI Tools:                          │
│                                              │
│  ✅ Claude Code v1.0.31                      │
│     ~/.claude/sessions/ (47 sessions)        │
│     [Enable]  [Configure]                    │
│                                              │
│  ✅ OpenAI Codex CLI v0.1.2                  │
│     ~/.codex/ (3 sessions)                   │
│     [Enable]  [Configure]                    │
│                                              │
│  ⬚ Aider (not installed)                     │
│     [Install Guide]                          │
│                                              │
│  ⬚ Gemini CLI (not installed)                │
│     [Install Guide]                          │
│                                              │
│  ➕ Add Custom Provider                       │
│                                              │
│                      [Continue →]            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 6. Hot-Reload & Config Merging

### Deep Merge Strategy (Like Superset's Overrides But Better)

```go
// MergeConfigs deep-merges user overrides onto builtin defaults.
// Rules:
// - Scalar fields: user value wins
// - Maps: deep merge (user keys overlay, builtin keys preserved)
// - Arrays: user value replaces entirely (no array merge ambiguity)
// - null/omitted: inherits builtin value
// - explicit null in YAML: clears the field
func MergeConfigs(builtin, override *ProviderConfig) *ProviderConfig {
    merged := deepCopy(builtin)
    applyOverrides(merged, override)
    return merged
}
```

### Hot-Reload via fsnotify

```go
func (r *Registry) startWatcher() {
    r.watcher, _ = fsnotify.NewWatcher()

    go func() {
        for event := range r.watcher.Events {
            if event.Op&(fsnotify.Write|fsnotify.Create) != 0 {
                if strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".yaml") {
                    r.reloadProvider(event.Name)
                    r.notifyFrontend("provider_config_changed")
                }
            }
        }
    }()

    r.watcher.Add(expandPath("~/.phantom-os/providers/"))
    r.watcher.Add(expandPath("~/.phantom-os/overrides/"))
    r.watcher.Add(expandPath("~/.phantom-os/providers/custom/"))
}
```

---

## 7. Frontend Integration

### Provider-Aware UI (SolidJS)

Instead of hardcoding "Claude" everywhere, the frontend receives provider metadata from the backend:

```typescript
// types.ts — provider info from backend
interface ProviderInfo {
  name: string;
  displayName: string;
  icon: string;
  enabled: boolean;
  installed: boolean;
  version: string | null;
  health: {
    installed: boolean;
    reachable: boolean;
    hasAuth: boolean;
  };
  commands: {
    resume: string;
    newSession: string;
    promptTransport: "argv" | "stdin" | "file" | "socket";
  };
  knownModels: string[];  // from pricing tiers
}

// Session cards show provider badge
interface Session {
  id: string;
  provider: string;        // "claude" | "codex" | "custom:my-llm"
  providerDisplay: string; // "Claude Code" | "OpenAI Codex CLI"
  // ... rest of session fields
}
```

### Provider Settings Page

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Settings > Providers                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                              │
│  Claude Code ✅                     [Edit]   │
│  v1.0.31 · 47 sessions · $12.34 total       │
│                                              │
│  OpenAI Codex CLI ✅                [Edit]   │
│  v0.1.2 · 3 sessions · $0.89 total          │
│                                              │
│  Aider ⬚                           [Setup]  │
│  Not installed                               │
│                                              │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────── │
│  Custom Providers                            │
│  ➕ Add from YAML    📦 Browse Marketplace   │
│                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 8. Comparison — Superset vs Phantom OS (Config-Driven)

| Dimension | Superset | Phantom OS (this design) |
|-----------|----------|--------------------------|
| **Agent definition** | TypeScript objects in source | YAML files on disk (embeddable in binary) |
| **Resolution chain** | builtin → override → custom | builtin YAML → user override YAML → custom YAML |
| **Config location** | In-app settings DB | `~/.phantom-os/providers/` (inspectable, version-controllable) |
| **Hot-reload** | No (requires app restart) | Yes (fsnotify on YAML files) |
| **Auto-detection** | No | Yes (scan paths + binaries) |
| **Custom providers** | Code-only (TypeScript) | YAML-only for simple tools, Go adapter for complex |
| **Prompt transport** | ✅ argv/stdin/file | ✅ argv/stdin/file/socket |
| **Session parsing** | None | Config-driven field mapping + Go adapters |
| **Token tracking** | None | Declarative field mapping in YAML |
| **Pricing** | None | YAML tiers with optional remote updates |
| **Health monitoring** | None | Binary + path + auth + version checks |
| **Multi-provider** | One per workspace | Unified timeline, all providers merged |
| **Community sharing** | None | YAML marketplace |
| **Override diffing** | ✅ Only stores diffs | ✅ Only stores diffs |
| **Zero-recompile changes** | N/A (TypeScript, needs rebuild) | ✅ YAML changes are instant |

---

## 9. Implementation Priority

Based on the research doc's 5-phase plan, updated with config-driven specifics:

| Phase | What | Key Deliverable | Days |
|-------|------|-----------------|------|
| **1a** | Config schema + `ConfigProvider` | `ProviderConfig` Go struct + YAML parser + generic field-mapped session discovery | 2 |
| **1b** | Extract `ClaudeProvider` | Move 17 critical coupling points behind adapter that embeds `ConfigProvider` | 3 |
| **2** | Registry + auto-detect + hot-reload | `Registry.LoadAll()`, `AutoDetect()`, fsnotify watcher | 2 |
| **3** | Frontend decoupling | Replace all 12 frontend coupling points with `ProviderInfo` from backend | 2 |
| **4** | Codex CLI as second provider | `codex.yaml` + optional Go adapter, multi-provider session merge | 3 |
| **5** | Community + marketplace | Custom provider UI, YAML validation, example configs | 2 |
| | **Total** | | **14 days** |

### Phase 1a is the safest starting point

It builds the config foundation without touching any existing code. The `ConfigProvider` can be tested independently with mock YAML configs before any refactoring begins.

---

## 10. Open Design Questions

1. **Should pricing tiers support regex or just substring match?** Substring is simpler but can't distinguish `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250514` from a hypothetical `sonnet-mini`. Regex adds complexity but is more precise.

2. **How to handle providers with no local persistence?** (Ollama, cloud APIs) — The `ConfigProvider` could create synthetic session files in `~/.phantom-os/sessions/{provider}/`, but this adds a state management concern.

3. **Should the YAML marketplace be centralized or federated?** A central GitHub repo is simplest (like Homebrew taps), but a federated model (each provider maintains their own config) is more sustainable.

4. **SQLite schema migration for multi-provider?** The current DB schema likely assumes single-provider. Need to add a `provider` column to sessions/messages tables.

5. **How to handle provider-specific UI?** Some providers may have unique features (Claude's thinking blocks, Codex's reasoning summaries). Should the frontend have provider-specific components, or normalize everything?

---

## 11. Real-World Provider Analysis

Based on direct inspection of actual filesystem artifacts on macOS (2026-04-26), here is what Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI really store.

### 11.1 Filesystem Layout

#### Claude Code (`~/.claude/`)

```
~/.claude/
  sessions/
    *.json                    # One file per session
  projects/
    <encoded-cwd>/            # CWD with / replaced by -
      *.jsonl                 # Conversation log (one JSON object per line)
  settings.json
  todos/
  tasks/
```

- **Session fields:** `pid`, `sessionId`, `cwd`, `model`, `startedAt`, `version`, `kind`, `entrypoint`, `status`
- **Conversation encoding:** JSONL (one JSON object per line)
- **Token format:** `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_read_input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens` — found in usage blocks inline with assistant messages
- **Message types:** `human`, `assistant`, `tool_use`, `tool_result`, `system`, `thinking`
- **CLI:** `/Users/subash.karki/.local/bin/claude`
- **Commands:** `claude --resume --session-id <id>`, `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`, `claude --print -p '<prompt>'`

#### Codex CLI (`~/.codex/`)

```
~/.codex/
  session_index.jsonl         # JSONL index: { id, thread_name, updated_at }
  state_5.sqlite              # SQLite DB with threads table
  sessions/
    YYYY/MM/DD/
      rollout-<timestamp>-<id>.jsonl   # Conversation (date-nested paths)
```

- **Dual discovery:** JSONL index (`session_index.jsonl`) **OR** SQLite (`state_5.sqlite` `threads` table)
- **SQLite `threads` columns:** `model_provider`, `model`, `cwd`, `title`, `tokens_used`, `source`, `approval_mode`, `git_sha`, `git_branch`
- **JSONL event types:** `session_meta`, `event_msg`, `response_item`, `turn_context` — completely different schema from Claude
- **Session meta payload:** `id`, `timestamp`, `cwd`, `originator`, `cli_version`, `source`, `model_provider`, `base_instructions`, `git`
- **Token format:** `tokens_used` aggregate in SQLite; per-event tokens in JSONL follow the OpenAI response format
- **CLI:** `/opt/homebrew/bin/codex`
- **Commands:** `codex --full-auto`, prompt via `codex --full-auto -- "prompt"`

#### Gemini CLI (`~/.gemini/`)

```
~/.gemini/
  tmp/
    <sha256-project-hash>/
      chats/
        session-*.json        # Full JSON file (NOT JSONL)
```

- **Session discovery:** Walk `tmp/<project-sha256-hash>/chats/session-*.json`
- **Conversation encoding:** Single JSON file with `sessionId`, `projectHash`, `startTime`, `lastUpdated`, `messages[]` array
- **Message format:** Each message has `id`, `timestamp`, `type`, `content` plus optional `thoughts[]`, `tokens`, `model`, `toolCalls[]`
- **Message types:** `user`, `gemini`, `info`, `error`
- **Token format:** Per-message `tokens: { input, output, cached, thoughts, tool, total }`
- **Tool calls:** `toolCalls[]` array per message with `id`, `name`, `args`, `result`, `status`
- **Thinking:** `thoughts[]` array per message with `subject`, `description`, `timestamp`
- **CLI:** `/opt/homebrew/bin/gemini`
- **Commands:** `gemini --approval-mode=auto_edit`

### 11.2 Format Comparison

| Dimension | Claude Code | Codex CLI | Gemini CLI |
|-----------|-------------|-----------|------------|
| **Session store** | Individual JSON files | JSONL index + SQLite DB | JSON files in hash-nested dirs |
| **Conversation encoding** | JSONL | JSONL (date-nested paths) | JSON (single file, `messages[]` array) |
| **Path convention** | CWD encoded (/ → -) | Date-nested (YYYY/MM/DD/) | SHA256 project hash |
| **Token tracking** | Per-line usage blocks | Aggregate in SQLite, per-event in JSONL | Per-message `tokens` object |
| **Message type names** | `human`, `assistant`, `thinking`, `tool_use`, `tool_result`, `system` | `session_meta`, `event_msg`, `response_item`, `turn_context` | `user`, `gemini`, `info`, `error` |
| **Tool call location** | Separate `tool_use` / `tool_result` JSONL lines | Inside `response_item` events | `toolCalls[]` array on message |
| **Thinking/reasoning** | Separate `thinking` type line | N/A (reasoning in response items) | `thoughts[]` array per message |
| **Session ID field** | `sessionId` | `id` (index) / thread ID (SQLite) | `sessionId` |
| **CWD field** | `cwd` | `cwd` (both index and SQLite) | Derived from `projectHash` |
| **Model field** | `model` | `model` (SQLite) / `model_provider` | `model` (per-message) |

### 11.3 Updated Recommendations

#### 3 Go Adapters Required (Not Config-Only)

The original design assumed config-only YAML could handle most providers. Real-world inspection reveals **all three major tools need Go adapters**:

| Provider | Why Go Adapter is Needed |
|----------|--------------------------|
| **Claude** | Encoded-path convention for conversation files; nested content blocks with mixed types; usage blocks interleaved in JSONL lines |
| **Codex** | Dual discovery (JSONL index + SQLite); date-nested conversation paths; completely different JSONL event schema (`session_meta`, `event_msg`, `response_item`, `turn_context`) |
| **Gemini** | SHA256 project hashes for path resolution; single JSON file (not JSONL) with `messages[]` array; per-message tokens/thoughts/toolCalls are embedded objects |

Config-only (pure YAML) remains viable for simpler tools (Aider, custom local LLMs) that use standard JSONL or flat JSON.

#### Content Extraction Strategy

The original design used JSONPath expressions like `content[*].text` for content extraction. This is too fragile — each provider's content is structurally different:

- **Claude:** Content blocks array where each block has a `type` discriminator (`text`, `tool_use`, `thinking`, etc.)
- **Codex:** OpenAI-format events where content is inside `response_item` payloads with their own `type` field
- **Gemini:** Direct `content` string on each message, with `toolCalls[]` and `thoughts[]` as sibling arrays

**Recommendation:** Replace JSONPath with an enum-based extraction strategy:

```yaml
conversations:
  content_extraction: "claude-blocks"  # "claude-blocks" | "openai-events" | "gemini-messages"
```

The Go adapter for each strategy knows how to extract text, tool calls, and thinking from its native format.

#### Token Tracking Variance

| Provider | Token Location | Granularity | Fields |
|----------|---------------|-------------|--------|
| **Claude** | Usage blocks in JSONL lines | Per-assistant-turn | `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_read_input_tokens`, `cache_creation_input_tokens` |
| **Codex** | SQLite `tokens_used` column + per-event in JSONL | Aggregate (SQLite) or per-event (JSONL) | OpenAI format |
| **Gemini** | `tokens` object on each message | Per-message | `input`, `output`, `cached`, `thoughts`, `tool`, `total` |

The `TokenUsage` normalized type must accommodate all three: input, output, cache_read, cache_write, thinking, tool, and total.

### 11.4 Updated YAML Schema (v1 Scope — ~40 Fields)

Based on Opus review feedback, the v1 schema is trimmed to essential fields only:

**Removed from v1 (deferred to v2+):**
- `auth` section (keychain, OAuth, config_file types)
- `safety.dangerous_patterns` (pattern matching)
- `pricing.remote_url` + `pricing.update_interval` (remote pricing sync)
- `conversations.content.text_paths` (replaced by `content_extraction` enum)
- Community marketplace support

**Added/Changed:**
- `conversations.encoding` now supports `"json"` (full file) in addition to `"jsonl"` and `"sqlite"`
- `conversations.content_extraction` enum replaces JSONPath-based content extraction
- `sessions.discovery_method` supports `"glob"`, `"jsonl_index"`, and `"sqlite"` for Codex's dual discovery
- `conversations.token_strategy` field: `"inline"` (Claude), `"aggregate"` (Codex SQLite), `"per-message"` (Gemini)

**v1 Field Count (~40):**

```yaml
# Identity (4 fields)
provider, display_name, icon, enabled

# Detection (4 fields)
detection.binary, detection.paths[], detection.version_command[], detection.version_pattern

# Paths (3 fields)
paths.sessions, paths.conversations, paths.settings

# Sessions (8 fields)
sessions.discovery_method, sessions.glob, sessions.exclude[],
sessions.fields.id, sessions.fields.pid, sessions.fields.cwd,
sessions.fields.model, sessions.fields.started_at
sessions.alive_check

# Conversations (10 fields)
conversations.encoding, conversations.file_extension, conversations.path_convention,
conversations.content_extraction, conversations.token_strategy,
conversations.message_types (6 normalized types mapped),
conversations.usage.locations[], conversations.usage.fields.{input,output,cache_read,cache_write}

# Commands (5 fields)
commands.resume, commands.new_session, commands.ai_generate,
commands.prompt_transport, commands.prompt_file_pattern

# Pricing (3 fields per tier + default_tier)
pricing.default_tier, pricing.tiers.{name}.{match, input_per_m, output_per_m, cache_read_per_m, cache_write_per_m}

# Adapter (2 fields)
adapter.go_package, adapter.overrides[]
```

### 11.5 Updated Provider Interface (5 Focused Interfaces)

Based on Opus review feedback, the monolithic `Provider` interface is split into 5 focused interfaces following the Interface Segregation Principle:

```go
// ProviderIdentity — who is this provider?
type ProviderIdentity interface {
    Name() string
    DisplayName() string
    Icon() string
    Enabled() bool
    IsInstalled() bool
    DetectedVersion() string
}

// SessionDiscoverer — how to find sessions
type SessionDiscoverer interface {
    DiscoverSessions(ctx context.Context) ([]RawSession, error)
    IsSessionAlive(session RawSession) bool
}

// ConversationParser — how to read conversations
type ConversationParser interface {
    FindConversationFile(sessionID, cwd string) (string, error)
    ParseConversation(r io.Reader) (*ConversationData, error)
}

// CostCalculator — how to calculate costs
type CostCalculator interface {
    ParseUsage(raw map[string]any) *TokenUsage
    CalculateCost(model string, usage TokenUsage) int64
}

// CommandRunner — how to interact with the CLI
type CommandRunner interface {
    ResumeCommand(sessionID string) string
    NewSessionCommand(cwd string) string
    AIGenerateCommand(prompt string) string
    PromptTransport() PromptTransport
}

// Provider composes all 5 focused interfaces
type Provider interface {
    ProviderIdentity
    SessionDiscoverer
    ConversationParser
    CostCalculator
    CommandRunner
    HealthCheck(ctx context.Context) HealthStatus
}
```

See `internal/provider/provider.go` for the full implementation with all normalized types.

### 11.6 DB Migration Note

In Phase 1b, the SQLite schema must be updated to support multi-provider sessions:

```sql
-- Add provider column to sessions table with backward-compatible default
ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN provider TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'claude';

-- Add provider to messages table for cross-provider queries
ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN provider TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'claude';

-- Index for provider-scoped queries
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_provider ON sessions(provider);
CREATE INDEX idx_messages_provider ON messages(provider);
```

The `DEFAULT 'claude'` ensures existing data (all from Claude Code) migrates cleanly without a backfill step.

## === docs/PLAN-home-redesign.md ===

# Home Screen Redesign — Mission Control

> Author: Subash Karki
> Created: 2026-04-24
> Status: Planning

---

## 1. Current State Analysis

### What exists (WorktreeHome.tsx — 735 lines)

The current home screen is a single-column vertical layout with:

1. **Workspace Status Card** — Branch name, clean/dirty indicator, ahead/behind counters, push/pull buttons, modified/untracked file counts. Well-built, functional.
2. **Pull Request Section** — Embedded in the status card below a divider. Shows current branch PR (with CI failure drill-down) or the open PR list for default branches. Includes "Create PR with Claude" action. This is the strongest section.
3. **Session Guard** — Conditionally shows active sessions with pause/resume/kill controls when ward system is enabled.
4. **Quick Actions** — 5 buttons (Terminal, New Session, Editor, Chat, Recipe). Three are "coming soon" placeholders.

### What's wrong

| Problem | Impact |
|---------|--------|
| **Single-column layout** | Content stacks vertically, leaving ~60% of the viewport as dead space (especially on wide monitors) |
| **No session stats** | Cost, tokens, context %, model — all tracked in the DB but invisible on the home screen |
| **No activity timeline** | The `activity_log` table exists with timestamps and types but is never shown here |
| **No tasks visibility** | Tasks are tracked per-session but hidden until you open a session pane |
| **No hunter profile** | The `hunter_profile`, `hunter_stats`, `achievements`, `daily_quests` tables exist in the schema but have NO Go bindings, NO SQL queries, and NO frontend signals. The DB tables are created but never populated. |
| **"Coming soon" buttons** | Three of five quick actions are dead — they add noise and signal incompleteness |
| **No commit history** | `getWorkspaceCommitLog` binding exists but the home screen doesn't use it |
| **Static feel** | No live-updating, no animations beyond the CI spinner. Feels like a settings page, not a cockpit. |

### What's good (keep these)

- The PR section with CI drill-down is excellent — keep and enhance
- The push/pull action buttons are well-designed
- The Session Guard / SessionControls component is solid
- The status card's glassmorphism border-glow aesthetic fits the vision
- The `home.css.ts` already has hunter banner, rank badge, and glow styles (hunterBanner, rankBadge, rankTitle, etc.) — they were built but never wired up

---

## 2. Data Availability Audit

### Ready to use (frontend bindings + signals exist)

| Data | Source | Signal/Binding |
|------|--------|----------------|
| Session list (active, paused, completed) | `sessions()` signal | Live via WebSocket events |
| Session cost (`estimated_cost_micros`) | Session object | Polled every 15s + WS updates |
| Session tokens (input, output, cache_read, cache_write) | Session object | Same |
| Session context % (`context_used_pct`) | Session object | Real-time via `session:context` event |
| Session model | Session object | Updated on enrichment |
| Session first prompt | Session object | Set on session creation |
| Session duration (`started_at`, `ended_at`) | Session object | Computed client-side |
| Task list per session | `getSessionTasks(sessionId)` | Binding exists, not surfaced on home |
| Activity log | `getActivityLog(sessionId, limit)` | Binding exists, not surfaced on home |
| Repo status (branch, clean, ahead, behind, staged, unstaged) | `getWorkspaceStatus(worktreeId)` | Already used on home |
| PR status + CI runs | `getPrStatus`, `getCiRuns`, `listOpenPrs` | Already used on home |
| Commit log | `getWorkspaceCommitLog(worktreeId, limit)` | Binding exists, not used on home |
| Branch list | `getProjectBranches(projectId)` | Available |
| Project recipes | `getProjectRecipes(projectId)` | Available |
| Git user name | `getGitUserName()` | Available |
| All worktree statuses | `getAllWorktreeStatus()` | Available |

### Needs new Go bindings + SQL queries

| Data | Table | What's Missing |
|------|-------|----------------|
| Hunter profile (rank, level, XP, streak) | `hunter_profile` | No SQL queries, no Go binding, no frontend type, no signal. Table has data columns but is never populated. |
| Hunter stats (STR, INT, AGI, VIT, PER, SEN) | `hunter_stats` | Same — fully orphaned |
| Achievements | `achievements` | Same |
| Daily quests | `daily_quests` | Same |
| Session cost aggregation (sum today/week/month) | `sessions` | No aggregation query. Can compute client-side from `sessions()` signal by summing `estimated_cost_micros` where `started_at` is today. |
| Activity log (global, not per-session) | `activity_log` | Query exists with optional `session_id` (passing NULL gets all). Binding `getActivityLog(sessionId, limit)` needs a wrapper that passes empty string for global. |

### Can compute client-side (no backend changes)

| Metric | Computation |
|--------|-------------|
| Total cost today | `sessions().filter(s => startedToday(s)).reduce((sum, s) => sum + (s.estimated_cost_micros ?? 0), 0) / 1_000_000` |
| Total tokens today | Same pattern for `input_tokens + output_tokens` |
| Active session count | `sessions().filter(s => s.status === 'active').length` |
| Active sessions in this worktree | Already computed as `worktreeSessions()` |
| Session duration | `Date.now()/1000 - s.started_at` |
| XP estimate | Based on sessions/tasks completed (mock until hunter system is wired) |

---

## 3. New Layout — CSS Grid Spec

### Grid Definition

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      HERO BAR                                │
│  Welcome back, Subash · Rank S · Streak 7d · XP 12,450     │
├─────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                     │                                        │
│   STAT CARDS        │    ACTIVE SESSIONS                     │
│   (2x2 mini grid)  │    (session list with context bars)     │
│   ┌─────┬─────┐    │                                        │
│   │ $4  │ 67% │    │                                        │
│   │cost │ ctx │    │                                        │
│   ├─────┼─────┤    │                                        │
│   │ 45k │opus │    │                                        │
│   │tkns │model│    │                                        │
│   └─────┴─────┘    │                                        │
│                     │                                        │
├─────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                     │                                        │
│   WORKSPACE +       │    RECENT ACTIVITY                     │
│   PULL REQUESTS     │    (timeline feed)                     │
│   (current card,    │                                        │
│    enhanced)        │                                        │
│                     │                                        │
├─────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                      QUICK ACTIONS                           │
│   [Terminal ⌘T] [New Session ⌘N] [Run Recipe ⌘R]           │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### CSS Grid Implementation

```css
.homeGrid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: minmax(280px, 1fr) minmax(320px, 2fr);
  grid-template-rows: auto 1fr 1fr auto;
  grid-template-areas:
    "hero       hero"
    "stats      sessions"
    "workspace  activity"
    "actions    actions";
  gap: 16px;
  padding: 24px;
  height: 100%;
  overflow-y: auto;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
```

### Responsive Breakpoints

| Width | Layout |
|-------|--------|
| > 900px | Full 2-column grid as shown above |
| 600-900px | Stack: hero, stats (2x2), sessions, workspace, activity, actions |
| < 600px | Single column, all sections stacked, stat cards become horizontal scroll |

Implementation: Use `@media` queries in vanilla-extract or container queries if the home pane has a fixed container.

---

## 4. Section-by-Section Design

### 4.1 Hero Bar

**Grid area**: `hero` (full width, top)

**Data source**: `getGitUserName()` for name, hunter_profile for rank/XP/streak (Phase 2), client-side computation for session stats summary.

**Phase 1 (no hunter system)**:
```
Welcome back, Subash  ·  3 active sessions  ·  $2.14 today  ·  142 tasks completed
```

**Phase 2 (with hunter system)**:
```
Welcome back, Hunter  ·  Rank: S  ·  XP: 12,450  ·  Streak: 7 days 🔥
```

**Design**:
- Full-width bar with `hunterBanner` style (already exists in `home.css.ts`)
- Rank badge on the left (already styled as `rankBadge`)
- Animated border glow (already defined as `borderGlow` keyframe)
- Orbitron display font for rank text
- Mono font for stats

**Animations**:
- Entrance: slide down + fade in (200ms ease-out)
- Rank badge: subtle pulse glow using existing `borderGlow` keyframe
- XP counter: count-up animation on mount (requestAnimationFrame)

**Component**: `HeroBanner.tsx`

### 4.2 Session Stat Cards

**Grid area**: `stats` (left column, row 2)

**Data source**: `sessions()` signal — all computation is client-side.

**Cards (2x2 mini-grid)**:

1. **Cost Today**
   - Value: Sum of `estimated_cost_micros` for sessions where `started_at` is today, divided by 1,000,000
   - Format: `$X.XX` (2 decimal places)
   - Icon: Dollar sign or credit card
   - Color: `vars.color.xp` (gold/amber)
   - Subtext: "estimated spend"

2. **Context Usage**
   - Value: `context_used_pct` of the most recent active session in this worktree
   - Format: Circular progress ring (SVG) with percentage in center
   - Color: Green < 50%, Amber 50-80%, Red > 80%
   - Subtext: session name or "no active session"

3. **Token Count**
   - Value: Sum of `input_tokens + output_tokens` for today's sessions
   - Format: `XX.Xk` with compact notation
   - Breakdown tooltip: input / output / cache_read / cache_write
   - Icon: Layers or hash
   - Color: `vars.color.mana` (blue/purple)
   - Subtext: "tokens today"

4. **Active Model**
   - Value: Model of the active worktree session (e.g., "opus", "sonnet")
   - Format: Badge with model name
   - Color: Different per model (opus = gold, sonnet = blue, haiku = green)
   - If multiple sessions: show most recent active
   - Subtext: session duration (e.g., "running 23m")

**Design per card**:
- GlassPanel base (`glass` style from `GlassPanel.css.ts`)
- Accent-colored top border (2px solid with glow)
- Large value text in `vars.font.display` (Orbitron)
- Small label in `vars.font.mono` uppercase
- Subtle hover: lift + increased glow

**Animations**:
- Entrance: stagger each card 50ms (fade in + scale from 0.95)
- Value changes: CSS transition on the number (smooth interpolation)
- Context ring: SVG `stroke-dashoffset` animation on mount and updates

**Component**: `StatCards.tsx` (contains 4 sub-components)

### 4.3 Active Sessions Panel

**Grid area**: `sessions` (right column, row 2)

**Data source**: `worktreeSessions()` memo (already exists) + `sessions()` for global view.

**Content**:
- Header: "Active Sessions" with count badge
- Toggle: "This worktree" / "All" (default: this worktree)
- Per session card:
  - Model badge (colored)
  - First prompt preview (truncated, 1 line)
  - Context bar: horizontal progress bar colored by threshold
  - Status: active (green dot) / paused (amber dot)
  - Duration: "running 23m" or "paused 5m ago"
  - Cost: "$0.42"
  - Action buttons: Pause/Resume, Kill, Jump to Terminal
- Empty state: "No active sessions — start one with ⌘N"

**Design**:
- Card with `statusCard` base style
- Session items use compact horizontal layout
- Context bar is a thin (4px) horizontal bar with gradient fill
- Model badge: pill-shaped with model-specific color
- Hover: highlight + show action buttons (hidden by default for clean look)

**Animations**:
- New session appears: slide in from top + fade
- Session ends: slide out + fade
- Context bar: smooth width transition (CSS transition 300ms)
- Status change (pause/resume): dot color transition

**Component**: `ActiveSessions.tsx`

### 4.4 Workspace + Pull Requests (Enhanced)

**Grid area**: `workspace` (left column, row 3)

**Data source**: Existing — `activeWorktree()`, `repoStatus()`, `prStatus()`, `ciSummary()`, `openPrs()`.

**Changes from current**:
- Keep all existing functionality (it's well-built)
- Add: Recent commits section (3 most recent, from `getWorkspaceCommitLog`)
  - Hash (short), subject, relative time
  - Click to expand full message
- Add: Branch quick-switch (dropdown of `getProjectBranches`)
- Add: Recipes section if project has recipes (`getProjectRecipes`)
- Visual: Use the existing card styles but add a subtle section divider between workspace info and PR info

**Design**: Minimal changes — this section is already well-designed. Just add commits below the PR section.

**Component**: Keep in `WorktreeHome.tsx` but extract workspace card into `WorkspaceCard.tsx`.

### 4.5 Recent Activity Timeline

**Grid area**: `activity` (right column, row 3)

**Data source**: `getActivityLog(null, 20)` — needs a small change to the binding to support global activity (pass empty session_id to get all).

**Content**:
- Header: "Recent Activity" with auto-refresh indicator
- Timeline of events, each with:
  - Timestamp (relative: "2m ago", "1h ago")
  - Event type icon (commit, PR, session start/end, branch switch, task complete)
  - Description text
  - Session attribution (which session triggered it)
- Auto-refreshes every 30s or on WebSocket events

**Event type → Icon mapping**:
| Type | Icon | Color |
|------|------|-------|
| `session_start` | Zap | `accent` |
| `session_end` | Square | `textDisabled` |
| `commit` | GitCommit | `success` |
| `pr_created` | GitPullRequest | `mana` |
| `pr_merged` | GitMerge | `mana` |
| `branch_switch` | GitBranch | `accent` |
| `task_completed` | CheckCircle | `success` |
| `tool_use` | Wrench | `textSecondary` |

**Design**:
- Vertical timeline with a thin accent-colored line on the left
- Each event is a small card with icon, text, and timestamp
- Alternating opacity for visual rhythm (latest = full opacity, older = dimmer)
- Scroll: max-height with overflow-y auto, custom scrollbar

**Animations**:
- New events: slide in from top with fade
- Timeline line: draws itself on mount (SVG animation)

**Component**: `ActivityTimeline.tsx`

### 4.6 Quick Actions (Redesigned)

**Grid area**: `actions` (full width, bottom)

**Changes**:
- Remove "coming soon" items entirely
- Keep: Terminal, New Session
- Add: Run Recipe (if project has recipes), Open in Editor (via `revealInFinder`)
- Each action card: icon + label + keyboard shortcut hint + 1-line description
- Horizontal flex row, wrapping

**Design**:
- Keep existing `quickActionButton` style but add description text
- Show keyboard shortcut as a subtle `<kbd>` badge
- Only 2-4 buttons (no dead weight)
- Wider cards with description beneath the label

**Component**: `QuickActions.tsx`

---

## 5. New Go Bindings Needed

### Phase 1 (can launch without these — compute client-side)

None required. All Phase 1 data is available from existing signals.

### Phase 2 (hunter system)

| Binding | SQL Query | Returns |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| `GetHunterProfile()` | `SELECT * FROM hunter_profile WHERE id = 1` | `HunterProfile` |
| `GetHunterStats()` | `SELECT * FROM hunter_stats WHERE id = 1` | `HunterStats` |
| `ListAchievements()` | `SELECT * FROM achievements ORDER BY unlocked_at DESC` | `[]Achievement` |
| `ListDailyQuests(date)` | `SELECT * FROM daily_quests WHERE date = ?` | `[]DailyQuest` |
| `GetTodaySessionStats()` | `SELECT COUNT(*) as count, SUM(estimated_cost_micros) as cost, SUM(input_tokens + output_tokens) as tokens FROM sessions WHERE started_at >= ? AND started_at < ?` | `SessionDayStats` |

### Phase 2 also needs

- Go logic to calculate and update XP on session end / task completion
- Go logic to evaluate streak (check `last_active_date` vs today)
- Go logic to check and unlock achievements
- Event emissions for hunter profile updates

### Binding for global activity log

The existing `getActivityLog(sessionId, limit)` needs a wrapper or overload where passing an empty `sessionId` returns global activity. The SQL query already supports this (`WHERE (session_id = ? OR ? IS NULL)`).

**New frontend binding**:
```typescript
export const getGlobalActivity = (limit: number) => getActivityLog('', limit);
```

Or add a new Go binding `GetGlobalActivityLog(limit int)` that passes NULL for session_id.

---

## 6. New SQL Queries Needed

```sql
-- queries/hunter.sql (new file)

-- name: GetHunterProfile :one
SELECT * FROM hunter_profile WHERE id = 1;

-- name: UpdateHunterXP :exec
UPDATE hunter_profile SET
    xp = ?, level = ?, rank = ?, title = ?,
    total_sessions = ?, total_tasks = ?,
    streak_current = ?, streak_best = ?,
    last_active_date = ?
WHERE id = 1;

-- name: GetHunterStats :one
SELECT * FROM hunter_stats WHERE id = 1;

-- name: ListUnlockedAchievements :many
SELECT * FROM achievements WHERE unlocked_at IS NOT NULL ORDER BY unlocked_at DESC;

-- name: GetDailyQuests :many
SELECT * FROM daily_quests WHERE date = ? ORDER BY quest_type;

-- name: GetSessionDayStats :one
SELECT
    COUNT(*) as session_count,
    COALESCE(SUM(estimated_cost_micros), 0) as total_cost_micros,
    COALESCE(SUM(input_tokens), 0) as total_input_tokens,
    COALESCE(SUM(output_tokens), 0) as total_output_tokens,
    COALESCE(SUM(cache_read_tokens), 0) as total_cache_read,
    COALESCE(SUM(cache_write_tokens), 0) as total_cache_write,
    COALESCE(SUM(tool_use_count), 0) as total_tool_uses,
    COALESCE(SUM(message_count), 0) as total_messages
FROM sessions
WHERE started_at >= ? AND started_at < ?;

-- name: GetGlobalActivityLog :many
SELECT * FROM activity_log
ORDER BY timestamp DESC
LIMIT ?;
```

---

## 7. Visual Design Specification

### Glassmorphism System

All cards use the GlassPanel base with per-section accent variations:

```css
/* Base glass */
background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--bgTertiary) 80%, transparent);
backdrop-filter: blur(16px);
border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) 20%, transparent);
border-radius: var(--radius-lg);

/* Accent top border for stat cards */
border-top: 2px solid var(--accent);
box-shadow: 0 -4px 16px color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) 15%, transparent);
```

### Glow Effects

| Element | Glow |
|---------|------|
| Hero banner | `borderGlow` keyframe (existing, 4s cycle) |
| Stat card hover | `box-shadow: 0 0 20px color-mix(in srgb, accent 25%, transparent)` |
| Context ring | SVG filter with `feGaussianBlur` on the progress stroke |
| Active session dot | `box-shadow: 0 0 8px` matching status color |
