| Timeline line | `background: linear-gradient(to bottom, accent, transparent)` |

### Progress Ring (Context %)

SVG-based circular progress indicator:
```
- Diameter: 48px
- Stroke width: 4px
- Background ring: vars.color.border (20% opacity)
- Progress ring: gradient based on value
  - 0-50%: vars.color.success
  - 50-80%: vars.color.warning
  - 80-100%: vars.color.danger
- Center text: percentage in vars.font.display
- Animation: stroke-dashoffset transition 500ms ease-out
```

### Color Tokens Per Section

| Section | Primary Color | Accent |
|---------|---------------|--------|
| Hero | `vars.color.accent` | `vars.color.rankGlow` |
| Cost card | `vars.color.xp` | `vars.color.xpMuted` |
| Context card | Dynamic (success/warning/danger) | Same, muted |
| Token card | `vars.color.mana` | `vars.color.manaMuted` |
| Model card | `vars.color.accent` | `vars.color.accentMuted` |
| Sessions | `vars.color.accent` | `vars.color.accentMuted` |
| Activity | `vars.color.textSecondary` | `vars.color.accent` (line) |
| Actions | `vars.color.accent` | `vars.color.accentGlow` (hover) |

### Typography

| Element | Font | Size | Weight |
|---------|------|------|--------|
| Hero greeting | `vars.font.display` | `vars.fontSize.lg` | 700 |
| Hero stats | `vars.font.mono` | `vars.fontSize.xs` | 400 |
| Stat card value | `vars.font.display` | `vars.fontSize.xl` | 700 |
| Stat card label | `vars.font.mono` | `vars.fontSize.xs` | 400, uppercase |
| Section title | `vars.font.mono` | `vars.fontSize.xs` | 400, uppercase, 0.12em tracking |
| Session name | `vars.font.body` | `vars.fontSize.sm` | 500 |
| Activity text | `vars.font.body` | `vars.fontSize.xs` | 400 |
| Activity time | `vars.font.mono` | `vars.fontSize.xs` | 400 |
| Quick action label | `vars.font.body` | `vars.fontSize.sm` | 500 |
| Quick action shortcut | `vars.font.mono` | `vars.fontSize.xs` | 400 |

---

## 8. Animation Specification

### Entrance Animations

| Element | Animation | Duration | Delay |
|---------|-----------|----------|-------|
| Hero bar | slideDown + fadeIn | 200ms | 0ms |
| Stat card 1 | fadeIn + scale(0.95→1) | 200ms | 50ms |
| Stat card 2 | fadeIn + scale(0.95→1) | 200ms | 100ms |
| Stat card 3 | fadeIn + scale(0.95→1) | 200ms | 150ms |
| Stat card 4 | fadeIn + scale(0.95→1) | 200ms | 200ms |
| Sessions panel | fadeIn + slideRight | 250ms | 100ms |
| Workspace card | fadeIn | 200ms | 150ms |
| Activity timeline | fadeIn + slideLeft | 250ms | 200ms |
| Quick actions | fadeIn + slideUp | 200ms | 250ms |

Easing: `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` (ease-out expo)

### Live Update Transitions

| Change | Animation |
|--------|-----------|
| Cost value change | Number tween (count up/down) over 300ms |
| Context % change | SVG stroke-dashoffset transition 500ms |
| New session appears | slideInFromTop 200ms + fadeIn |
| Session removed | fadeOut 150ms + collapse height 200ms |
| New activity event | slideInFromTop 200ms |
| Token count change | Number tween 300ms |
| Status dot change | color transition 300ms |

### Keyframes to Add

```typescript
// home-grid.css.ts

const fadeInUp = keyframes({
  from: { opacity: 0, transform: 'translateY(8px)' },
  to: { opacity: 1, transform: 'translateY(0)' },
});

const fadeInScale = keyframes({
  from: { opacity: 0, transform: 'scale(0.95)' },
  to: { opacity: 1, transform: 'scale(1)' },
});

const slideInRight = keyframes({
  from: { opacity: 0, transform: 'translateX(-12px)' },
  to: { opacity: 1, transform: 'translateX(0)' },
});

const slideInLeft = keyframes({
  from: { opacity: 0, transform: 'translateX(12px)' },
  to: { opacity: 1, transform: 'translateX(0)' },
});

const drawLine = keyframes({
  from: { height: '0%' },
  to: { height: '100%' },
});
```

---

## 9. Component Breakdown

### New Files to Create

```
frontend/src/components/panes/home/
├── HomeGrid.tsx              # Main grid container (replaces WorktreeHome content)
├── HomeGrid.css.ts           # Grid layout + responsive styles
├── HeroBanner.tsx            # Welcome bar with rank/stats
├── HeroBanner.css.ts
├── StatCards.tsx              # 2x2 stat card grid
├── StatCards.css.ts
├── ContextRing.tsx           # SVG circular progress for context %
├── ContextRing.css.ts
├── ActiveSessions.tsx        # Live session list with controls
├── ActiveSessions.css.ts
├── ActivityTimeline.tsx       # Recent activity feed
├── ActivityTimeline.css.ts
├── QuickActions.tsx           # Redesigned action buttons
├── QuickActions.css.ts
├── WorkspaceCard.tsx          # Extracted from WorktreeHome (workspace + PR)
└── WorkspaceCard.css.ts
```

### Modified Files

```
frontend/src/components/panes/WorktreeHome.tsx
  → Becomes a thin wrapper that renders <HomeGrid />
  → Keeps the NewWorktreeDialog and worktree/project memos
  → Passes data down as props

frontend/src/styles/home.css.ts
  → Keep existing styles (they're still used by sub-components)
  → Add new grid-level styles

frontend/src/core/bindings/sessions.ts
  → Add getGlobalActivityLog(limit) wrapper
```

### Type Additions (core/types/index.ts)

```typescript
export interface HunterProfile {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  level: number;
  xp: number;
  xp_to_next: number;
  rank: string;
  title: string;
  total_sessions: number;
  total_tasks: number;
  total_repos: number;
  streak_current: number;
  streak_best: number;
  last_active_date: string | null;
  created_at: number;
}

export interface HunterStats {
  strength: number;
  intelligence: number;
  agility: number;
  vitality: number;
  perception: number;
  sense: number;
}

export interface Achievement {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string | null;
  icon: string | null;
  category: string | null;
  xp_reward: number;
  unlocked_at: number | null;
}

export interface DailyQuest {
  id: string;
  date: string;
  quest_type: string;
  label: string;
  target: number;
  progress: number;
  completed: number;
  xp_reward: number;
}
```

---

## 10. Phase Breakdown

### Phase 1 — Grid Layout + Stat Cards + Session List (MVP)

**Goal**: Fill the empty space with useful, live data using ONLY existing signals.

**Tasks**:
1. Create `HomeGrid.tsx` with CSS Grid layout
2. Create `HeroBanner.tsx` — greeting with git username + session count + cost today (all client-side computed from `sessions()`)
3. Create `StatCards.tsx` — 4 glass cards (cost, context, tokens, model) — all from `sessions()` signal
4. Create `ContextRing.tsx` — SVG circular progress component
5. Create `ActiveSessions.tsx` — session list with context bars, pause/resume/kill
6. Extract `WorkspaceCard.tsx` from existing WorktreeHome (workspace status + PRs, move as-is)
7. Refactor `WorktreeHome.tsx` to render `<HomeGrid />`
8. Remove "coming soon" quick actions, keep only Terminal + New Session
9. Add entrance animations
10. Responsive layout (media queries)

**Backend changes**: None
**Risk**: Low — purely frontend, using existing data

### Phase 2 — Activity Timeline + Commits + Recipes

**Goal**: Add historical context and recent commit visibility.

**Tasks**:
1. Add `getGlobalActivityLog` binding wrapper (or new Go binding)
2. Create `ActivityTimeline.tsx` — event feed with icons and timestamps
3. Add recent commits section to `WorkspaceCard` (using `getWorkspaceCommitLog`)
4. Add recipe launcher to Quick Actions (using `getProjectRecipes`)
5. Auto-refresh activity on timer + WebSocket events
6. Polish timeline animations

**Backend changes**: Possibly one new Go binding for global activity log
**Risk**: Low

### Phase 3 — Hunter System Integration

**Goal**: Wire up the gamification tables that already exist in the schema.

**Tasks**:
1. Write SQL queries for hunter_profile, hunter_stats, achievements, daily_quests
2. Write Go bindings for all hunter queries
3. Write Go logic to calculate and update XP on session events
4. Create frontend types + signals for hunter data
5. Update HeroBanner to show real rank, XP, streak
6. Add XP gain animation (floating +XP numbers)
7. Add streak flame indicator
8. Daily quest widget (stretch goal)

**Backend changes**: Significant — new queries, bindings, business logic
**Risk**: Medium — requires Go + DB work

### Phase 4 — Polish + Advanced Features

**Tasks**:
1. CI/CD overview section (expandable workflow runs)
2. Keyboard shortcuts for all quick actions
3. Drag-to-reorder sections (user layout customization)
4. Section collapse/expand (persist to preferences)
5. Dark/light mode animation transitions
6. Performance optimization (virtualized lists for many sessions)
7. Sound effects on session events (optional, controlled by preference)

---

## 11. Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Layout system | CSS Grid | Native, performant, clean responsive behavior |
| State management | SolidJS signals (existing) | Already reactive, no new deps needed |
| Chart/progress | Raw SVG | Tiny footprint, full control, matches aesthetic |
| Animations | CSS keyframes + transitions | No JS animation library needed, GPU-accelerated |
| Data fetching | Client-side computation from signals | Phase 1 needs zero backend changes |
| Scrolling | Per-section overflow | Each section manages its own scroll, grid doesn't scroll |
| Coming soon items | Remove entirely | Dead buttons signal incompleteness — only show what works |

---

## 12. Success Criteria

- [ ] Zero empty space on a 1440px-wide viewport
- [ ] All data is live-updating (no stale stats)
- [ ] Page load to fully rendered < 100ms (all data is from signals, no new API calls in Phase 1)
- [ ] Works on all 12 theme variants (tokens, not hardcoded colors)
- [ ] Responsive down to 600px width
- [ ] No new dependencies added
- [ ] Entrance animations complete within 500ms total
- [ ] Every section answers a developer question at a glance

## === docs/PLAN-phase-1-upgrades.md ===

# PLAN — Phase 1 Upgrades (steals from agent-deck + agentchattr)

**Author:** Subash Karki
**Date:** 2026-04-29
**Source roadmap:** `/Users/subash.karki/phantom-os/v2/UPGRADE_ROADMAP.md`
**Reality-checked against:** v2 working tree at this date.

This plan turns Phase 1 of the roadmap into 6 separately-shippable PRs. Each is grounded in actual file paths and existing patterns in v2 — not speculative.

---

## Reality checks (corrections to UPGRADE_ROADMAP.md)

| Roadmap claim | Reality | Plan adjustment |
|---|---|---|
| Use `vars.color.error` for status dot | Theme has `danger`, not `error` (`theme.css.ts:27,68,101…`) | Use `vars.color.danger` / `vars.color.dangerMuted` |
| Status enum is `active` only | DB has `active \| completed \| paused` (`internal/db/queries/sessions.sql:11,14`) — many queries pin on `active` | Don't widen DB column. Add a derived `liveState` field to the wire event only |
| Need new `session:status` event | `session:stale` already exists (`internal/collector/events.go:11`) | Reuse: stale → maps to `idle`. Add `session:waiting` for tool-use-pending |
| MCP config has list/toggle | `mcp_config.go` only registers/unregisters *phantom-ai*; reads/writes `~/.mcp.json` and `~/.claude/settings.json` | Add `ListMCPServers()` + `ToggleMCPServer()` in same file or `mcp_list.go` |
| WorktreeItem needs new dot | `WorktreeItem.tsx:111` already has `<span class={styles.sessionDot} />` | Color it by state via data attribute, no new element |
| Add new sub-interface for Fork | `FindConversationFile` already on `ConversationParser` interface (`provider.go:162`) | Extend `CommandRunner` (line 177) instead of new interface |

---

## PR 1 — Status semantics (running / waiting / idle / error)

**Effort:** S (~1 day)
**Roadmap item:** Phase 1 #3
**Outcome:** Sidebar shows colored dot per session state. No DB migration. No breaking change to existing `active`/`completed`/`paused`.

### Design

- **DB column unchanged.** `sessions.status` keeps `active | completed | paused`.
- **New derived state** computed in `session_watcher.go` and emitted on `session:update`:
  - `running` — last activity < 2s
  - `waiting` — JSONL has trailing `tool_use` with no matching `tool_result` (assistant blocked on user)
  - `idle` — `5s < last_activity < 5min` AND DB status = `active`
  - `error` — most recent JSONL line has `type:"error"` or DB status flips with non-zero exit
  - (terminal `completed` keeps a gray-quiet dot)
- **New event:** none. Extend payload of existing `EventSessionUpdate` with `live_state: string` field.

### Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `internal/collector/session_watcher.go` | Add `computeLiveState(jsonlPath, dbStatus, lastActivityAt)` helper. Call it before `emitEvent("session:update", …)`. Add the value to the event payload map. |
| `internal/collector/events.go` | No new constants needed (reuse `EventSessionUpdate`). Add a one-line doc note about the `live_state` field. |
| `frontend/src/core/types/index.ts` | Add `live_state?: 'running' \| 'waiting' \| 'idle' \| 'error'` to `Session`. |
| `frontend/src/core/signals/sessions.ts` | Update `SessionUpdateEvent` interface to carry `live_state`. Merge into store on update. |
| `frontend/src/components/sidebar/WorktreeItem.tsx` | Add `data-live-state={session()?.live_state}` to the existing `<span class={styles.sessionDot}>` at line 111. |
| `frontend/src/components/sidebar/<WorktreeItem.css.ts>` (sibling file) | Add color rules: `[data-live-state="running"]` → `vars.color.success`, `waiting` → `vars.color.warning`, `idle` → `vars.color.fgMuted`, `error` → `vars.color.danger`. Add 1.4s breathing animation on `running`. |

### Acceptance

1. Start a Claude session in a worktree → dot turns green within 2s.
2. Trigger an approval prompt (e.g., file edit gate) → dot turns yellow within debounce window.
3. Leave session idle 10s → dot turns gray.
4. Force a JSONL with a trailing error line → dot turns red.
5. `make go-test` passes; existing `session_watcher_test.go` tests still green.

### Risks

- JSONL tool-use detection might be expensive. Mitigation: only re-classify on file mod (already debounced 200ms), and only read the last few lines via `tail` semantic (`io.SeekEnd`).
- Other queries (`gamification.sql`, `journal.sql`) read `status='completed'` — confirmed unaffected because we don't touch the DB column.

---

## PR 2 — Slash commands in ChatPane

**Effort:** S (~½ day)
**Roadmap item:** Phase 1 #2
**Outcome:** `/clear`, `/summary`, `/help` work in chat input. Foundation for Phase 2's `/continue`.

### Design

In `ChatPane.tsx:473` `handleSend`, intercept input that starts with `/`. Dispatch to a small dispatcher map. Show a `/help` popover above the textarea when input is exactly `/`.

Slash commands for Phase 1 (only the cheap ones — defer chain-related to Phase 3):

| Command | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `/clear` | Calls existing `DeleteMessagesByConversation` via `core/bindings/chat.ts`. Refreshes message list. |
| `/help` | Local-only popover listing registered commands. No backend. |
| `/copy` | Copies the last assistant message text to clipboard via `navigator.clipboard.writeText`. |

Defer to Phase 2: `/summary` (needs `SummarizeConversation` Go method), `/continue` (needs Loop Guard).

### Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `frontend/src/components/panes/ChatPane.tsx` | In `handleSend`: early-return on leading `/` with a switch over commands. Show inline `/help` popover when `input().trim() === '/'`. |
| `frontend/src/styles/chat.css.ts` (or sibling) | Small style for the popover (existing `attachmentBar` pattern). |

### Acceptance

1. Type `/clear` + Enter → conversation messages cleared, no error toast.
2. Type `/` → small popover shows the 3 registered commands; ESC dismisses.
3. Type `/copy` after assistant reply → clipboard contains last reply.
4. Type `/unknown` → toast "Unknown command: /unknown" and input preserved.

### Risks

- None. One-file change, fully local.

---

## PR 3 — Image lightbox

**Effort:** S (~½ day)
**Roadmap item:** Phase 1 #5
**Outcome:** Click any inline image in chat → full-screen modal viewer.

### Design

New shared component `ImageLightbox/` modeled on existing `PhantomDrawer/`-style overlays. Uses `PhantomModal` (per `coding-principles.md`: never raw Kobalte `Dialog`). Captures click on `<img>` inside `MarkdownContent`.

### Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `frontend/src/shared/ImageLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` | New. Props: `src`, `alt`, `open`, `onClose`. Wraps `PhantomModal`. |
| `frontend/src/shared/ImageLightbox/ImageLightbox.css.ts` | Backdrop, max-w/max-h, ESC handling via Kobalte. |
| `frontend/src/shared/ImageLightbox/index.ts` | Re-export. |
| `frontend/src/components/panes/ChatPane.tsx` | In `MarkdownContent` (line 171), add a `ref` callback that wires `<img>` `click` handlers to set a local `lightbox()` signal. Render `<ImageLightbox …>` at root. |

### Acceptance

1. Paste a screenshot → send → click thumbnail → lightbox opens.
2. ESC closes; click outside closes; click image inside does NOT close (only X / ESC / outside).
3. Lightbox respects current theme (uses `vars.color.bg…`).

### Risks

- `MarkdownContent` is sanitized via DOMPurify. Confirm `onclick` is not stripped — use `addEventListener` after sanitization, not inline attrs.

---

## PR 4 — `llms-full.txt` + `Makefile docs-llm` target

**Effort:** S (~2 hours)
**Roadmap item:** Phase 1 #6
**Outcome:** Single-file machine-readable project digest at repo root, regeneratable via `make docs-llm`.

### Design

Concatenate a curated subset of `docs/*.md` into `llms-full.txt`. Include header pointers to authoritative files instead of inlining everything. Keep total under ~200 KB.

Curated subset:
- `docs/ai-engine.md`
- `docs/DESIGN-config-driven-architecture.md`
- `docs/PLAN-home-redesign.md`
- `README.md`
- This plan (`docs/PLAN-phase-1-upgrades.md`)

### Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `Makefile` | New target `docs-llm` after `frontend-typecheck`. Uses `cat` with section headers. Writes to `./llms-full.txt`. |
| `llms-full.txt` | Generated. Add to `.gitignore`? (decide in PR — recommend committing for now so external agents can fetch via raw URL after the repo goes public.) |
| `README.md` | Add a "For agents" section linking to the file. |

### Acceptance

1. `make docs-llm` produces `llms-full.txt` < 200 KB.
2. File starts with `# PhantomOS v2 — agent context` and includes table of contents.
3. Reading the file alone gives a Claude/GPT enough to answer "how is the provider abstraction structured?".

### Risks

- Concat order matters for skimming. Lock it in the Makefile.

---

## PR 5 — MCP Manager UI

**Effort:** M (~2 days — bigger than roadmap estimate)
**Roadmap item:** Phase 1 #1 (the easy one), revised to M
**Outcome:** Dialog under Cmd+K → "Toggle MCP servers" lists every server in `~/.mcp.json` with a switch.

### Why M not S

Original roadmap assumed `mcp_config.go` already had list/toggle. It only has `RegisterPhantomMCP` / `UnregisterPhantomMCP` for phantom-ai itself (`mcp_config.go:73,139`). Listing arbitrary servers needs new code.

### Design

Backend reads `~/.mcp.json` (the file Claude Code looks at) and `~/.claude/settings.json` `enabledMcpjsonServers` array. UI shows: server name, command, args summary, enabled toggle. Toggle flips the entry in the `enabledMcpjsonServers` array — no MCP child restart needed (Claude re-reads on next session).

Scope cycle (LOCAL/GLOBAL) deferred — v2 only has GLOBAL today, and the project-scope concept needs design. Ship GLOBAL-only.

### Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `internal/integration/mcp_list.go` | New. `ListMCPServers() ([]MCPServer, error)`, `ToggleMCPServer(name string, enabled bool) error`. Reuses helpers from `mcp_config.go` (`os.UserHomeDir`, JSON read/write). |
| `internal/app/bindings_mcp.go` | New. Wails methods: `ListMCPServers`, `ToggleMCPServer`. Wire to `App` struct in `app.go`. |
| `frontend/src/core/bindings/mcp.ts` | New thin wrapper. |
| `frontend/src/core/bindings/index.ts` | Export `mcp`. |
| `frontend/src/core/signals/mcp.ts` | New. `mcpServers` resource signal. |
| `frontend/src/shared/McpManagerDialog/McpManagerDialog.tsx` | New. `PhantomModal`-based, Kobalte `Switch` per row, type-to-filter. |
| `frontend/src/shared/McpManagerDialog/McpManagerDialog.css.ts` | New. List + row styles. |
| `frontend/src/shared/McpManagerDialog/index.ts` | Re-export. |
| `frontend/src/shared/CommandPalette/actions.ts` | Add a new entry to `SESSION_ACTIONS` (line ~245) — "Toggle MCP servers" — opening the dialog via a signal. |
| `frontend/src/core/signals/mcp.ts` | Also exports `mcpManagerOpen` boolean signal + `openMcpManager()` / `closeMcpManager()`. |
| `frontend/src/app.tsx` | Mount `<McpManagerDialog />` at root. |

### Acceptance

1. Cmd+K → "Toggle MCP servers" → dialog opens listing all servers from `~/.mcp.json`.
2. Flip a switch → file on disk reflects the change in `enabledMcpjsonServers` array.
3. Restart `wails dev` → toggle state persists.
4. If `~/.mcp.json` missing, dialog shows empty state with a "Register phantom-ai" button calling existing `RegisterPhantomMCP`.

### Risks

- Concurrent edits to `~/.claude/settings.json` from Claude Code itself. Mitigation: read-modify-write with file lock (`flock` on Unix). Acceptable race window since user is unlikely to edit both at once.
- Schema drift in `~/.mcp.json`. Be permissive on read (any unknown fields kept and round-tripped).

---

## PR 6 — Session Fork

**Effort:** M (~3 days)
**Roadmap item:** Phase 1 #1 in roadmap (the flagship)
**Outcome:** Cmd+Shift+F (or palette → "Fork session") creates a new conversation that inherits transcript, opens a new pane.

### Design

The Claude provider already has `FindConversationFile` (`claude.go:49`). Fork = read source JSONL, write to a new path under the same encoded-CWD directory with a new UUID, register as a session via the existing watcher pickup path.

### Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `internal/provider/provider.go:177` | Extend `CommandRunner` interface with `ForkConversation(sessionID, newName string) (newSessionID string, err error)`. |
| `internal/provider/config_provider.go` | Default impl: copy file, generate UUID, return new ID. Works for any provider that uses CWD-encoded layout (Claude, future providers). |
| `internal/provider/claude/claude.go` | No override needed — base impl from `ConfigProvider` is sufficient. Add a unit test verifying the new file lands under the encoded-CWD dir. |
| `internal/db/queries/sessions.sql` | New query `:exec` `CreateSession` already exists (line 19). Re-use. New query `CopySessionForFork` not needed — `ForkConversation` calls `CreateSession` with cloned fields + new ID + `parent_session_id`. |
| `internal/db/migrations/` | New migration adding nullable `parent_session_id TEXT REFERENCES sessions(id)`. Allows tracking fork tree. |
| `internal/app/bindings_session_fork.go` | New. Wails method `ForkSession(sessionID, name string) (newID string, err error)`. Resolves provider via existing `bindings_provider.go` pattern. |
| `frontend/src/core/bindings/sessions.ts` | Add `forkSession(id, name)` wrapper. |
| `frontend/src/core/signals/sessions.ts` | `forkSession()` action — calls binding, optimistically inserts new session, lets `session:new` event reconcile. |
| `frontend/src/shared/CommandPalette/actions.ts:245` | Add "Fork session" action under `SESSION_ACTIONS`. Disabled when no active session. Default shortcut `Cmd+Shift+F`. |
| `frontend/src/shared/ForkSessionDialog/` | Optional. v0 can skip the dialog and use the active session's name + " (fork)". Add the dialog only if naming friction emerges. |
| `frontend/src/components/panes/ChatPane.tsx` | Header gets a small "Fork" icon button (lucide `GitFork`) calling `forkSession()`. |

### Acceptance

1. Open a chat with N messages → palette → "Fork session" → new pane opens with same N messages.
2. Type a new message in the fork → original session unchanged.
3. JSONL on disk has 2 files for the same `<encoded-cwd>` dir; new file's first line matches old file's first line.
4. `parent_session_id` column populated for the new row.
5. Fork-of-fork works (cycle in the tree).

### Risks

- Provider plurality: only Claude tested. Codex provider may differ in transcript layout. Plan: gate the action on `provider.SupportsFork()` (new method, false for non-Claude providers initially).
- Disk space on large transcripts. Mitigation: hardlink instead of copy where same filesystem (`os.Link`). Fall back to copy.
- Concurrent fork while watcher is reading → file truncation race. Mitigation: write to `<id>.tmp` then `os.Rename`.

---

## Sequencing & branch hygiene

**Recommended order** (small → big, also derisks tooling):

1. **PR 4** (`llms-full.txt`) — gives Claude reading this codebase later more grip. ½ day.
2. **PR 2** (Slash commands) — purely frontend, no migrations. ½ day.
3. **PR 3** (Image lightbox) — purely frontend, introduces shared ImageLightbox. ½ day.
4. **PR 1** (Status semantics) — touches collector + frontend signals. 1 day.
5. **PR 5** (MCP Manager UI) — first feature touching `~/.mcp.json` + `~/.claude/settings.json`. 2 days.
6. **PR 6** (Session Fork) — schema migration + provider interface. 3 days.

Each PR ships independently. Branch from `main`, rebase between PRs only if there's a dependency (PR 1 has no dep; PR 6 has no hard dep on PR 1 but UI feels better when status dots already work).

**Total estimate:** ~7–8 working days for one developer. Realistic timeline: 1.5–2 weeks with reviews and Wails rebuilds.

---

## Cross-cutting requirements

Per `.claude/rules/coding-principles.md`:

- Every new `.go`/`.ts`/`.tsx` source file: header `// Author: Subash Karki`.
- All new dialogs: wrap `PhantomModal`, never raw Kobalte `Dialog`.
- All new buttons: `buttonRecipe({ variant, size })` from `recipes.css.ts`.
- Inputs: Kobalte `TextField`, not raw `<input>`.
- Colors: `vars.color.*` (`success`, `warning`, `danger`, `fgMuted`, etc.) — never hex.
- Co-locate `*.css.ts` with components.
- After SQL changes (PR 6): `~/go/bin/sqlc generate`.
- Phantom-AI integration: before editing any of the listed files, call `mcp__phantom-ai__phantom_graph_context` per the global instruction.

---

## What's NOT in Phase 1

Deferred to Phase 2/3 and tracked in `UPGRADE_ROADMAP.md`:

- @mention autocomplete + agent pills (P2)
- Channels within a conversation (P2)
- Multi-select message delete (P2)
- Conversation summaries — `/summary` command (P2)
- Cost dashboard + budgets (P2)
- Skills Manager (P2)
- Loop-guarded agent-to-agent chain (P3)
- Watchers (GitHub/webhook) (P3)
- Conductor (persistent supervisor) (P3)
- Export/Import zip (P3)

---

## Suggested first action

Start with **PR 4** (`llms-full.txt`) — 2 hours, no risk, and improves every subsequent agent task in this repo (including the rest of these PRs).

Or, if you want a more visible "ah, that's better" win, start with **PR 2** (slash commands) — same effort, instantly noticeable in the chat pane.

Pick one and say "let's start PR N" — I'll execute against this plan.
